events.desc_zh: 宇宙從極度高溫高密度的奇點爆炸膨脹，時間、空間與物質同時誕生。
events.desc_en: The universe exploded from an infinitely dense singularity, creating space, time, and matter.
events.amazon_title: A Brief History of Time — Stephen Hawking

events.desc_zh: 宇宙大爆炸後約2億年，第一批恆星在氫氦雲中點燃，結束宇宙黑暗時代。
events.desc_en: About 200 million years after the Big Bang, the first stars ignite in clouds of hydrogen and helium.

events.desc_zh: 我們的銀河系逐漸形成，包含超過2000億顆恆星，直徑約10萬光年。
events.desc_en: Our Milky Way galaxy gradually assembles, containing over 200 billion stars.

events.desc_zh: 太陽由星際氣體雲坍縮形成，周圍塵埃盤逐漸凝聚成八大行星。
events.desc_en: The Sun condenses from a molecular cloud; surrounding dust forms the planets.

events.desc_zh: 地球由太陽系早期微行星不斷碰撞積聚而成，初期地表為熔融岩漿海洋。
events.desc_en: Earth accretes from colliding planetesimals; its surface is an ocean of molten magma.
events.amazon_title: A Short History of Nearly Everything — Bill Bryson

events.desc_zh: 地球最早期，地表熔融，隕石轟炸頻繁，大氣以水蒸氣、二氧化碳為主，無生命。
events.desc_en: Earth's earliest period: molten surface, frequent meteorite bombardment, no life.

events.desc_zh: 一顆火星大小的天體「忒伊亞」撞擊早期地球，噴出碎片凝聚成月球。
events.desc_en: A Mars-sized body called Theia collides with early Earth; ejected debris forms the Moon.

events.desc_zh: 最早的岩石與最原始生命（原核生物）出現，大氣無氧，藍藻開始演化。
events.desc_en: Earliest rocks and primitive prokaryotic life appear; oxygen-free atmosphere; cyanobacteria evolve.

events.desc_zh: 最早的單細胞原核生物在海洋中出現，開啟地球生命38億年的歷程。
events.desc_en: The earliest single-celled prokaryotic life emerges in Earth's oceans, beginning 3.8 billion years of life.

events.desc_zh: 大氧化事件、多次雪球地球冰河期、真核細胞與多細胞生物出現，生命急速複雜化。
events.desc_en: Great Oxidation Event, Snowball Earth glaciations, eukaryotes and multicellular life appear.

events.desc_zh: 地球史上最早、最嚴重的冰河期之一，由大氧化事件導致溫室氣體甲烷破壞而引發，持續3億年。
events.desc_en: One of Earth's earliest and most severe glaciations, triggered by the Great Oxidation Event destroying methane greenhouse gas; lasted 300 million years.

events.desc_zh: 藍藻光合作用產生大量氧氣積累在大氣中，造成厭氧生物大滅絕，卻為複雜生命鋪路。
events.desc_en: Cyanobacteria produce vast oxygen that accumulates in the atmosphere, killing anaerobic life but enabling complex organisms.

events.desc_zh: 具有細胞核的真核細胞出現，這是生命複雜化的關鍵一步，奠定所有高等生物的基礎。
events.desc_en: Cells with a true nucleus emerge, a critical leap toward complex life and the ancestor of all higher organisms.

events.desc_zh: 最早的多細胞生物演化出現，細胞開始分工合作，為複雜動植物的演化奠基。
events.desc_en: The first multicellular organisms evolve; cells begin to specialize, laying the groundwork for complex life.

events.desc_zh: 地球幾乎完全被冰雪覆蓋，全球平均溫度降至零下50度，海洋表面結冰，持續近1億年。
events.desc_en: Earth almost entirely frozen; global temperatures drop to -50°C; ocean surfaces freeze for nearly 100 million years.

events.desc_zh: 生命從海洋到陸地的偉大征程，包含寒武紀大爆發、魚類時代、第一批陸地植物與動物，以二疊紀大滅絕終結。
events.desc_en: Life's great journey from sea to land: Cambrian explosion, Age of Fishes, first land plants and animals, ending with the Permian extinction.

events.desc_zh: 幾乎所有現代動物門在短短數百萬年內同時出現，三葉蟲稱霸海洋，是生命演化的大爆發時代。
events.desc_en: Nearly all major animal phyla appear simultaneously; trilobites dominate the seas; the explosion of complex life.

events.desc_zh: 海洋生物空前繁盛，珊瑚礁出現，末期發生地球第二大滅絕事件（奧陶紀大滅絕），85%物種消失。
events.desc_en: Marine life flourishes; coral reefs appear; ends with Earth's second-largest extinction (85% of species lost).

events.desc_zh: 有頜魚類演化出現，陸地植物開始定居，珊瑚礁大量發展，氣候相對溫和穩定。
events.desc_en: Jawed fish evolve; land plants colonize shore; coral reefs expand; relatively warm and stable climate.

events.desc_zh: 「魚類的時代」——各類魚種繁盛，四足動物登陸，蕨類森林出現，末期發生大滅絕。
events.desc_en: 'Age of Fishes' — diverse fish species; tetrapods colonize land; fern forests appear; major extinction at end.

events.desc_zh: 巨大沼澤森林覆蓋大陸，大量植物埋入地層形成今日煤礦，第一批爬蟲類與有翅昆蟲出現。
events.desc_en: Vast swamp forests cover continents; buried plants form today's coal deposits; first reptiles and winged insects appear.

events.desc_zh: 盤古大陸形成，以史上最大滅絕事件結束——96%海洋物種與70%陸地脊椎動物消失。
events.desc_en: Pangaea forms; ends with Earth's greatest extinction — 96% of marine species and 70% of land vertebrates lost.

events.desc_zh: 地球史上最大規模物種滅絕事件，96%海洋物種與70%陸地脊椎動物滅絕，幾乎終結地球生命。
events.desc_en: Earth's greatest mass extinction: 96% of marine species and 70% of land vertebrates wiped out.
events.amazon_title: When Life Nearly Died — Michael Benton

events.desc_zh: 恐龍主宰大地的時代，包含三疊紀、侏羅紀、白堊紀，哺乳類與鳥類也在此期間演化出現。
events.desc_en: The age of dinosaurs: Triassic, Jurassic, and Cretaceous periods; mammals and birds also evolve.

events.desc_zh: 二疊紀大滅絕後生命復甦，恐龍與哺乳類同時演化出現，盤古大陸開始分裂。
events.desc_en: Life recovers after Permian extinction; dinosaurs and mammals both evolve; Pangaea begins breaking apart.

events.desc_zh: 恐龍稱霸陸地，超巨型蜥腳類恐龍出現，始祖鳥演化，盤古大陸持續分裂，氣候溫暖濕潤。
events.desc_en: Dinosaurs dominate land; giant sauropods appear; Archaeopteryx evolves; warm and humid climate.

events.desc_zh: 開花植物大爆發，恐龍多樣性達到頂峰，以隕石撞擊引發的大滅絕事件結束。
events.desc_en: Flowering plants explode in diversity; dinosaurs reach peak diversity; ends with asteroid impact extinction.

events.desc_zh: 直徑10公里小行星撞擊墨西哥猶加敦半島，非鳥類恐龍全部滅絕，哺乳類趁機崛起。
events.desc_en: A 10km asteroid strikes the Yucatán Peninsula; non-avian dinosaurs extinct; mammals rise to dominance.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/白堊紀-古近紀滅絕事件

events.desc_zh: 哺乳類與鳥類大輻射演化，草原擴張，靈長類出現，最終演化出人類，延續至今。
events.desc_en: Mammals and birds diversify explosively; grasslands expand; primates evolve; culminates in humans.

events.desc_zh: 南方古猿開始直立行走，解放雙手，為大腦發展和工具使用創造條件。
events.desc_en: Australopithecus begins walking upright, freeing the hands for tool use and enabling brain expansion.

events.desc_zh: 260萬年前開始、至今仍在持續的冰河期，包含多次冰期與間冰期，人類在此期間演化。
events.desc_en: Ongoing ice age beginning 2.6 million years ago, with multiple glacial-interglacial cycles; humans evolved during this period.

events.desc_zh: 能人製造最早的石器工具（奧杜萬工業），標誌人類技術文明的起點。
events.desc_en: Homo habilis creates the earliest stone tools (Oldowan industry), marking the dawn of human technology.

events.desc_zh: 直立人學會控制和使用火，改變飲食方式，促進大腦發育，並提供保護。
events.desc_en: Homo erectus controls fire, transforming diet, promoting brain development, and providing protection.

events.desc_zh: 現代智人在非洲演化出現，腦容量1400毫升，語言與抽象思維能力大幅提升。
events.desc_en: Modern Homo sapiens evolves in Africa with a 1400ml brain, capable of language and abstract thought.

events.desc_zh: 最後一次冰河期，冰蓋覆蓋北美、歐洲大部，海平面下降120公尺，人類通過白令陸橋進入美洲。
events.desc_en: Last glacial period; ice sheets cover North America and Europe; sea level drops 120m; humans cross into Americas via Bering land bridge.

events.desc_zh: 智人大規模走出非洲，向亞洲、歐洲、澳洲擴散，逐漸取代其他人屬物種。
events.desc_en: Homo sapiens migrates out of Africa en masse, spreading to Asia, Europe, and Australia, replacing other hominins.
events.amazon_title: The Journey of Man — Spencer Wells

events.desc_zh: 人類開始創作洞穴壁畫（如法國拉斯科），展現抽象思維和藝術表達能力。
events.desc_en: Humans create cave paintings (e.g. Lascaux, France), demonstrating abstract thinking and artistic expression.

events.desc_zh: 臺東長濱八仙洞遺址，是目前所知臺灣最古老的文化，距今約三萬年，以打製石器為特徵。
events.desc_en: The Changbin Culture at Baxian Cave in Taitung is Taiwan's oldest known culture (~30,000 years ago), characterized by chipped stone tools.

events.desc_zh: 臺南左鎮出土的人類化石，是迄今發現最早的臺灣人類遺骸，距今約兩萬年。
events.desc_en: Human fossil remains found in Zuozhen, Tainan, the earliest human remains discovered in Taiwan (~20,000 years ago).

events.desc_zh: 人類在新月沃土開始種植小麥大麥，在中國種植稻米，從遊牧轉為定居農耕，文明由此展開。
events.desc_en: Humans begin cultivating wheat and barley in the Fertile Crescent, rice in China; civilization begins.
events.amazon_title: Guns, Germs, and Steel — Jared Diamond

events.desc_zh: 臺灣新石器時代早期文化，已有農耕與陶器，被認為是南島語族的祖先文化。
events.desc_en: Taiwan's early Neolithic culture with farming and pottery, considered the ancestral culture of Austronesian peoples.

events.desc_zh: 語言學與遺傳學研究顯示，臺灣可能是南島語族的發源地，其後裔擴散至太平洋、印度洋各島嶼。
events.desc_en: Linguistic and genetic research suggests Taiwan may be the homeland of Austronesian peoples, whose descendants spread across the Pacific and Indian Oceans.

events.desc_zh: 蘇美人在底格里斯河與幼發拉底河流域建立城市文明，發明楔形文字，建造神廟。
events.desc_en: The Sumerians build urban civilizations between the Tigris and Euphrates rivers, inventing cuneiform writing and constructing ziggurats.

events.desc_zh: 哈拉帕和摩亨佐-達羅等城市興起，擁有先進的城市規劃和排水系統。
events.desc_en: Cities like Harappa and Mohenjo-daro flourish with advanced urban planning and sophisticated drainage systems.

events.desc_zh: 中國第一個朝代，大禹建立，奠定中華文明基礎。
events.desc_en: China's first dynasty, founded by Yu the Great.

events.desc_zh: 臺灣北部金屬器時代文化，已使用鐵器，被認為是凱達格蘭族的祖先。
events.desc_en: Iron Age culture in northern Taiwan, believed to be ancestors of the Ketagalan people.

events.desc_zh: 巴比倫王漢摩拉比頒布世界上最早的成文法律之一，包含282條法律條文。
events.desc_en: Babylonian King Hammurabi promulgates one of the earliest written legal codes, comprising 282 laws.

events.desc_zh: 商朝創立甲骨文，青銅器文化達到頂峰，是有文字記載的最早朝代。
events.desc_en: The Shang Dynasty saw the creation of oracle bone script and the peak of Bronze Age culture.

events.desc_zh: 臺灣原住民族多達16族，發展出豐富多元的文化，在平原、山地與東部各自建立部落社會。
events.desc_en: Over 16 indigenous tribes develop rich and diverse cultures across Taiwan's plains, mountains, and eastern coast.

events.desc_zh: 希臘城邦聯合攻打特洛伊城，這場傳奇戰爭被荷馬記錄於《伊里亞德》中。
events.desc_en: Greek city-states unite to besiege Troy, a legendary conflict immortalized by Homer in the Iliad.

events.desc_zh: 周武王滅商，建立封建制度，禮樂文明大盛。
events.desc_en: Zhou Wuwang overthrew the Shang and established the feudal system.

events.desc_zh: 荷馬創作《伊里亞德》和《奧德賽》，奠定西方文學的基礎。
events.desc_en: Homer composes the Iliad and Odyssey, foundational works of Western literature.

events.desc_zh: 周室衰微，諸侯爭霸，孔子、老子等百家思想興起。
events.desc_en: Decline of Zhou; rise of competing states and the Hundred Schools of Thought.

events.desc_zh: 周室東遷後諸侯林立，春秋五霸、戰國七雄先後稱霸。
events.desc_en: After Zhou moved east, rival states dominated: Five Hegemons in Spring & Autumn, Seven Kingdoms in Warring States.

events.desc_zh: 孔子創立儒家學說，深刻影響中國及東亞文明兩千年。
events.desc_en: Confucius founds Confucianism, shaping East Asian civilization for 2,500 years.

events.desc_zh: 克利斯提尼在雅典建立世界上最早的民主制度，公民直接參與城邦治理。
events.desc_en: Cleisthenes establishes the world's first democracy in Athens, where citizens directly participate in governance.

events.desc_zh: 釋迦牟尼在菩提樹下悟道，創立佛教，影響後來亞洲文明超過兩千五百年。
events.desc_en: Siddhartha Gautama attains enlightenment under the Bodhi Tree, founding Buddhism, which would shape Asian civilization for millennia.

events.desc_zh: 希臘城邦聯盟抵抗波斯帝國入侵，薩拉米斯海戰成為決定性勝利，保護了西方文明。
events.desc_en: Greek city-states repel the Persian Empire's invasion; the Battle of Salamis is a decisive victory protecting Western civilization.

events.desc_zh: 七雄並立，諸子百家爭鳴，兵家、法家、縱橫家興盛。
events.desc_en: Seven major states vie for dominance; military strategy and philosophy flourish.

events.desc_zh: 馬其頓的亞歷山大大帝東征，建立橫跨歐亞非的帝國，促進希臘文化與東方文化的交融。
events.desc_en: Alexander the Great conquers a vast empire from Greece to India, spreading Greek culture and facilitating East-West cultural exchange.

events.desc_zh: 水稻農業傳入日本，金屬工具出現，日本進入農耕文明。
events.desc_en: Rice agriculture arrives in Japan; metal tools appear; Japan enters agricultural civilization.

events.desc_zh: 羅馬共和國透過布匿戰爭擊敗迦太基，逐步成為地中海地區的霸主。
events.desc_en: Rome defeats Carthage in the Punic Wars, gradually establishing dominance over the entire Mediterranean region.

events.desc_zh: 秦始皇統一六國，建立中央集權制度，統一文字度量衡，修建長城。
events.desc_en: Qin Shi Huang unifies China, standardizes writing, weights, and builds the Great Wall.
events.amazon_title: The Early Chinese Empires — Mark Edward Lewis

events.desc_zh: 漢朝奠定中華民族主體，絲路開通，儒學立為國教，造紙術成熟。
events.desc_en: The Han Dynasty shapes Chinese national identity, opens the Silk Road, and adopts Confucianism as state doctrine.

events.desc_zh: 漢武帝派張騫出使西域，開拓絲綢之路，連接東西方，促進商業、文化與思想的交流。
events.desc_en: Emperor Wu of Han sends Zhang Qian westward, opening the Silk Road connecting East and West, enabling trade in silk, spices, and ideas.

events.desc_zh: 尤利烏斯·凱撒在羅馬元老院被刺殺，引發內戰，最終導致羅馬共和國終結。
events.desc_en: Julius Caesar is assassinated in the Senate, triggering civil war and ultimately ending the Roman Republic.

events.desc_zh: 耶穌基督的誕生成為西方曆法紀元的起點，基督教信仰逐漸擴展至整個羅馬帝國。
events.desc_en: The birth of Jesus Christ marks the epoch of the Western calendar; Christianity gradually spreads throughout the Roman Empire.

events.desc_zh: 魏蜀吳三國鼎立，諸葛亮、曹操等人物成為千古傳奇。
events.desc_en: Wei, Shu, and Wu compete for dominance; legendary figures like Zhuge Liang and Cao Cao.

events.desc_zh: 大和政權統一日本，天皇制度確立，佛教自朝鮮傳入。
events.desc_en: Yamato state unifies Japan; imperial system established; Buddhism introduced from Korea.

events.desc_zh: 晉武帝短暫統一，後分裂為五胡十六國，北方動盪。
events.desc_en: Brief reunification under Jin Wudi, followed by fragmentation and the Sixteen Kingdoms in the north.

events.desc_zh: 北方五個少數民族建立十六個政權，與東晉南北對峙。
events.desc_en: Five northern ethnic groups establish sixteen states; coexist with Eastern Jin in the south.

events.desc_zh: 南北對峙，佛教大興，北魏孝文帝漢化改革。
events.desc_en: North-south division; Buddhism flourishes; Emperor Xiaowen of Northern Wei promotes Sinicization.

events.desc_zh: 西羅馬帝國在日耳曼蠻族的入侵下滅亡，標誌著古代史結束、中世紀開始。
events.desc_en: The Western Roman Empire falls to Germanic tribes, marking the end of antiquity and the beginning of the Middle Ages.

events.desc_zh: 隋文帝再次統一中國，開鑿大運河，科舉制度奠基。
events.desc_en: Reunification of China; Grand Canal constructed; imperial examination system established.

events.desc_zh: 唐朝是中國歷史黃金時代，長安為當時世界最大城市，詩詞藝術達到頂峰。
events.desc_en: Tang Dynasty is China's golden age; Chang'an is the world's largest city; poetry and arts flourish.

events.desc_zh: 穆罕默德創立伊斯蘭教，從麥地那向外傳播，在數十年內橫跨中東、北非與中亞。
events.desc_en: Muhammad founds Islam in Medina; within decades it spreads across the Middle East, North Africa, and Central Asia.
events.amazon_title: A History of the Muslim World: From Its Origins to the Dawn of Modernity

events.desc_zh: 奈良建都，唐文化影響深遠；平安時代假名文字誕生，源氏物語等文學名著問世。
events.desc_en: Nara capital built under Tang influence; Heian era sees kana script and The Tale of Genji.

events.desc_zh: 北歐維京人開始大規模航海探險，劫掠、貿易和殖民，最遠到達北美洲。
events.desc_en: Norse Vikings begin extensive sea voyages for raiding, trading, and colonization, reaching as far as North America.

events.desc_zh: 唐朝滅亡後北方五朝更迭，南方十國並立，動盪分裂。
events.desc_en: After the Tang, five dynasties succeed in the north while ten kingdoms coexist in the south.

events.desc_zh: 宋朝活字印刷、火藥、指南針廣泛應用，商業繁榮，是科技黃金時代。
events.desc_en: Song Dynasty sees widespread use of movable type, gunpowder, and compass; commercial and technological golden age.

events.desc_zh: 教皇烏爾班二世號召基督徒奪回聖地，數次十字軍東征促進了東西方文化交流。
events.desc_en: Pope Urban II calls Christians to reclaim the Holy Land; the Crusades significantly accelerate cultural exchange between East and West.

events.desc_zh: 南宋偏安江南，金國控北方，蒙古崛起前的三國並立局面。
events.desc_en: Southern Song in the south, Jin in the north, and rising Mongols — three powers before Yuan unification.

events.desc_zh: 鎌倉幕府建立，武士階級崛起，日本進入武家政治時代。
events.desc_en: Kamakura shogunate established; samurai class rises; Japan enters warrior-governed era.

events.desc_zh: 成吉思汗統一蒙古各部，建立史上最大的連續陸地帝國，橫跨歐亞大陸。
events.desc_en: Genghis Khan unifies the Mongol tribes and builds the largest contiguous land empire in history, spanning from East Asia to Eastern Europe.

events.desc_zh: 蒙古忽必烈建立元朝，統治全中國，馬可波羅來訪，東西方交流空前。
events.desc_en: Kublai Khan establishes Yuan; Marco Polo visits; unprecedented East-West exchange.

events.desc_zh: 鼠疫從中亞蔓延至歐洲，奪走歐洲三分之一至二分之一的人口，深刻改變歐洲社會結構。
events.desc_en: Bubonic plague spreads from Central Asia to Europe, killing a third to half of Europe's population and transforming medieval society.

events.desc_zh: 明朝建立，鄭和七下西洋，萬里長城達今日形態，北京紫禁城建成。
events.desc_en: Ming Dynasty established; Zheng He's voyages; Great Wall takes modern form; Forbidden City built.

events.desc_zh: 文藝復興在義大利興起，人文主義思想復甦，達文西、米開朗基羅等藝術大師輩出。
events.desc_en: The Renaissance begins in Italy, reviving humanism and producing towering figures like Leonardo da Vinci and Michelangelo.

events.desc_zh: 約翰尼斯·古騰堡發明活字印刷術，使書籍大量生產成為可能，知識的傳播速度革命性提升。
events.desc_en: Johannes Gutenberg invents the movable-type printing press, enabling mass production of books and revolutionizing the spread of knowledge.

events.desc_zh: 馬丁·路德發表《九十五條論綱》，引發宗教改革，打破天主教在歐洲的宗教壟斷。
events.desc_en: Martin Luther posts his 95 Theses, triggering the Protestant Reformation and breaking the Catholic Church's religious monopoly in Europe.

events.desc_zh: 臺灣中部超部落聯盟，由巴布拉族與巴則海族組成，曾抵抗荷蘭與鄭氏政權，1732年遭清朝鎮壓瓦解。
events.desc_en: A confederation of tribes in central Taiwan, resisting Dutch and Ming-Zheng rule; dissolved after Qing suppression in 1732.

events.desc_zh: 哥白尼提出日心說，推翻地心說，開啟科學革命，人類對宇宙的認識根本改變。
events.desc_en: Copernicus proposes the heliocentric model, overturning geocentrism and sparking the Scientific Revolution.

events.zh: 葡萄牙人命名「福爾摩沙」
events.desc_zh: 葡萄牙航海者航經臺灣海峽，驚嘆其美景而命名「Ilha Formosa（美麗之島）」，此名沿用至今。
events.desc_en: Portuguese sailors, passing through the Taiwan Strait, name the island 'Ilha Formosa' (Beautiful Isle), a name still used today.

events.desc_zh: 德川幕府建立，鎖國政策，和平繁榮265年，浮世繪、歌舞伎等文化繁盛。
events.desc_en: Tokugawa shogunate; sakoku isolation policy; 265 years of peace; ukiyo-e and kabuki culture flourish.

events.desc_zh: 荷蘭東印度公司佔據臺灣西南部，建熱蘭遮城，引進農業技術與外來物種，開始系統化開發臺灣。
events.desc_en: The Dutch East India Company occupies southwestern Taiwan, builds Fort Zeelandia, introduces agriculture and trade, systematically developing the island.

events.desc_zh: 西班牙佔領雞籠（基隆）與淡水，建聖薩爾瓦多城，1642年被荷蘭人驅逐。
events.desc_en: Spain occupies Keelung and Tamsui, builds Fort San Salvador; expelled by the Dutch in 1642.

events.desc_zh: 滿族建立清朝，康雍乾三朝鼎盛，後遭列強入侵，鴉片戰爭開啟近代屈辱史。
events.desc_en: Manchu Qing Dynasty peaks under three emperors, then faces Western imperialism and the Opium Wars.

events.desc_zh: 鄭成功驅逐荷蘭人，在臺建立第一個漢人政權，推行屯田政策，大量漢人移民來臺。
events.desc_en: Koxinga (Zheng Chenggong) expels the Dutch, establishes Taiwan's first Han Chinese government, and promotes Han immigration.

events.desc_zh: 施琅攻台，明鄭降清。清朝統治212年，臺灣從福建省一府三縣逐步發展為獨立建省，人口大增。
events.desc_en: Shi Lang's naval victory ends Ming-Zheng rule. Qing governs Taiwan for 212 years, eventually making it a separate province.

events.desc_zh: 牛頓發表《自然哲學的數學原理》，建立經典力學體系，統一了地上與天上的物理規律。
events.desc_en: Newton publishes Principia Mathematica, establishing classical mechanics and unifying terrestrial and celestial physics.

events.desc_zh: 英國率先發生工業革命，蒸汽機改變生產方式，人類文明進入工業化時代。
events.desc_en: Britain leads the Industrial Revolution; the steam engine transforms production and ushers humanity into the industrial age.

events.desc_zh: 法國大革命推翻君主制，提出自由、平等、博愛的理念，深刻影響世界民主化進程。
events.desc_en: The French Revolution overthrows the monarchy, proclaiming liberty, equality, and fraternity, profoundly influencing global democratization.

events.desc_zh: 達爾文發表《物種起源》，提出天擇演化論，徹底改變人類對生命起源的理解。
events.desc_en: Darwin publishes On the Origin of Species, proposing natural selection and fundamentally changing humanity's understanding of life.

events.desc_zh: 明治維新推行現代化改革，脫亞入歐，日本迅速崛起為亞洲強國。
events.desc_en: Meiji Restoration modernizes Japan along Western lines, rapidly making Japan a major power.

events.desc_zh: 清法戰爭後，臺灣從福建省獨立出來成為臺灣省，劉銘傳就任首任巡撫，積極推動現代化建設。
events.desc_en: After the Sino-French War, Taiwan becomes a separate province; Liu Mingchuan becomes its first governor and modernizes the island.

events.desc_zh: 甲午戰爭清朝戰敗，與日本簽訂馬關條約，將臺灣、澎湖割讓予日本，臺灣民主國短暫抵抗失敗。
events.desc_en: China loses the First Sino-Japanese War and cedes Taiwan to Japan via the Treaty of Shimonoseki; the Taiwan Republic briefly resists.

events.desc_zh: 日本統治臺灣50年，推行現代化建設（鐵路、醫療、教育），也實施殖民壓迫與皇民化政策。
events.desc_en: Japan rules Taiwan for 50 years, modernizing infrastructure (railways, medicine, education) while implementing colonial oppression and Japanization policies.

events.desc_zh: 孫中山推翻清朝，建立中華民國，結束兩千年帝制。
events.desc_en: Sun Yat-sen overthrows the Qing, founding the Republic of China and ending imperial rule.

events.desc_zh: 第一次世界大戰爆發，超過1700萬人死亡，四大帝國瓦解，重塑世界政治格局。
events.desc_en: World War I erupts, killing over 17 million people, dissolving four empires and reshaping the global political order.

events.desc_zh: 布爾什維克革命推翻沙皇政權，建立蘇聯，共產主義思想席捲世界。
events.desc_en: The Bolshevik Revolution overthrows the Tsar and establishes the Soviet Union, spreading communist ideology worldwide.

events.desc_zh: 五四運動推動科學與民主，是中國近代史重要轉折點。
events.desc_en: The May Fourth Movement promotes science and democracy, a pivotal turning point in modern Chinese history.

events.desc_zh: 賽德克族首領莫那魯道率族人武裝抗日，是日治時期最大規模的原住民武裝起義。
events.desc_en: Seediq chief Mona Rudao leads an armed uprising against Japanese rule, the largest indigenous resistance during Japanese occupation.

events.desc_zh: 第二次世界大戰造成超過7000萬人死亡，是人類歷史上最大規模的武裝衝突。
events.desc_en: World War II kills over 70 million people, becoming the deadliest armed conflict in human history.

events.desc_zh: 美國在廣島和長崎投下原子彈，二戰結束，人類進入核武器時代。
events.desc_en: The US drops atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki, ending WWII and ushering in the nuclear age.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/廣島與長崎原子彈爆炸

events.desc_zh: 二戰後51個國家簽署聯合國憲章，成立聯合國，致力於維護世界和平與安全。
events.desc_en: 51 nations sign the UN Charter after WWII, founding the United Nations to maintain international peace and security.

events.desc_zh: 日本在二戰中戰敗投降，隨後在美國協助下推行民主改革，創造經濟奇蹟。
events.desc_en: Japan surrenders in WWII, then undergoes democratic reform under US guidance and achieves economic miracle.

events.desc_zh: 1947年2月28日爆發的省民反抗國民政府事件，遭武力鎮壓，數萬人罹難，影響臺灣社會深遠。
events.desc_en: On Feb. 28, 1947, an uprising against the KMT government is violently suppressed; tens of thousands die, leaving a deep scar on Taiwanese society.

events.desc_zh: 毛澤東在天安門宣布中華人民共和國成立，中國進入新時代。
events.desc_en: Mao Zedong proclaims the PRC from Tiananmen, beginning a new era in Chinese history.

events.desc_zh: 國共內戰失利後，中華民國政府播遷臺灣，帶來大量軍民與文物，開始長期統治臺灣。
events.desc_en: After losing the Civil War, the ROC government retreats to Taiwan, bringing large numbers of soldiers, civilians, and cultural artifacts.

events.desc_zh: 中華民國宣布戒嚴，長達38年，是世界上持續時間最長的戒嚴之一，限制人民自由與政治活動。
events.desc_en: Martial law lasting 38 years — one of the world's longest — restricts civil liberties and political activities.

events.desc_zh: 沃森與克里克發現DNA雙螺旋結構，開創分子生物學，奠定現代生物技術的基礎。
events.desc_en: Watson and Crick discover the DNA double helix, founding molecular biology and laying the groundwork for modern biotechnology.

events.desc_zh: 臺灣從農業經濟快速轉型為出口導向工業，成為亞洲四小龍之一，人均所得大幅提升。
events.desc_en: Taiwan rapidly transforms from agriculture to export-oriented industry, becoming one of the Four Asian Tigers with dramatically rising living standards.

events.desc_zh: 蔣經國宣布解除戒嚴，臺灣開始民主化歷程，開放黨禁、報禁，走向自由民主社會。
events.desc_en: Chiang Ching-kuo lifts martial law, beginning Taiwan's democratization: lifting bans on political parties and press freedom.

events.desc_zh: 張忠謀創立台灣積體電路製造公司（台積電），開創晶圓代工模式，成為全球半導體產業核心。
events.desc_en: Morris Chang founds TSMC, pioneering the semiconductor foundry model; TSMC becomes the world's most critical chipmaker.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/台灣積體電路製造公司

events.desc_zh: 提姆·伯納斯-李發明全球資訊網，網際網路向公眾開放，人類進入資訊時代。
events.desc_en: Tim Berners-Lee invents the World Wide Web, opening the internet to the public and ushering in the Information Age.

events.desc_zh: 臺灣舉行首次總統直接選舉，李登輝當選，是華人社會第一次民主直選總統，具有重大歷史意義。
events.desc_en: Taiwan holds its first direct presidential election; Lee Teng-hui wins in a historic milestone for Chinese-speaking democracies.

events.desc_zh: 人類基因組計畫完成，完整解讀人類約30億個DNA鹼基對，開啟精準醫療新時代。
events.desc_en: The Human Genome Project completes the full sequence of human DNA, opening a new era of precision medicine.

events.desc_zh: 蘋果公司發布第一代iPhone，智慧型手機普及，行動網路改變人類生活方式。
events.desc_en: Apple releases the first iPhone, smartphones become ubiquitous, and mobile internet transforms how humanity lives and connects.

events.desc_zh: ChatGPT 等大型語言模型向公眾開放，人工智慧技術進入爆炸性發展階段，深刻改變各行各業。
events.desc_en: Large language models like ChatGPT become publicly available, triggering an AI revolution that begins transforming every industry.

events.desc_zh: 大爆炸後10⁻³²秒，宇宙從量子尺度急速膨脹至宏觀尺度，奠定宇宙大尺度結構。
events.desc_en: 10⁻³² seconds after the Big Bang, the universe expands exponentially from quantum to macroscopic scale.

events.desc_zh: 大爆炸後38萬年，電子與質子結合成氫原子，宇宙從不透明變為透明，宇宙微波背景輻射釋放。
events.desc_en: 380,000 years after the Big Bang, electrons combine with protons; the universe becomes transparent; the CMB is released.
events.amazon_title: The First Three Minutes — Steven Weinberg

events.desc_zh: 宇宙復合後到第一批恆星誕生前，宇宙中沒有任何光源，完全黑暗。
events.desc_en: Between recombination and the first stars, the universe contained no light sources — completely dark.

events.desc_zh: 早期恆星聚集，第一批原始星系在宇宙中形成，引力成為宇宙結構的主宰力量。
events.desc_en: Early stars cluster together; the first primitive galaxies form; gravity becomes the dominant force shaping cosmic structure.

events.desc_zh: 太陽系最大行星率先形成，其強大引力主導太陽系結構，保護內行星免受彗星撞擊。
events.desc_en: The solar system's largest planet forms first; its gravity dominates the solar system structure and shields inner planets.

events.desc_zh: 第二大氣態巨行星形成，以壯觀的環狀系統聞名，主要由冰和岩石組成。
events.desc_en: The second gas giant forms, famous for its spectacular ring system made of ice and rock.

events.desc_zh: 地球冷卻後，大氣中水蒸氣凝結形成最早的液態海洋，生命出現的先決條件。
events.desc_en: As Earth cools, water vapor condenses to form the first liquid oceans — a prerequisite for life.

events.desc_zh: 約40億年前，大量小行星密集撞擊地球與月球，可能同時帶來大量水和有機分子。
events.desc_en: ~4 billion years ago, intense asteroid bombardment; may have delivered water and organic molecules to Earth.

events.desc_zh: 最早的大陸地殼碎片形成，格陵蘭發現的阿卡斯塔片麻岩是目前已知最古老的岩石。
events.desc_en: The earliest continental crust fragments form; Acasta Gneiss in Canada is the oldest known intact rock.

events.desc_zh: 地球板塊漂移活動開始，大陸不斷碰撞分裂，塑造今日地球的地貌與山脈。
events.desc_en: Plate tectonic activity begins; continents drift, collide, and split, shaping today's geography and mountains.

events.desc_zh: 已知最早有完整記錄的超大陸，幾乎包含當時所有陸塊，後來分裂成多個板塊。
events.desc_en: The earliest well-documented supercontinent, containing nearly all landmasses; later broke apart into multiple plates.

events.desc_zh: 藍藻形成的疊層石是最早生命留下的岩石記錄，在澳洲皮爾巴拉發現，距今35億年。
events.desc_en: Stromatolites formed by cyanobacteria are the earliest rock record of life, found in Pilbara, Australia.

events.desc_zh: 雪球地球結束後，最早的複雜多細胞生物出現，軟體動物，無骨骼，是動物演化的先驅。
events.desc_en: After Snowball Earth, the first complex multicellular organisms appear — soft-bodied, boneless pioneers of animal evolution.

events.desc_zh: 最早的苔蘚類植物從海洋登上陸地，開始改造大氣成分，為陸地動物的到來鋪路。
events.desc_en: The earliest moss-like plants colonize land, begin transforming the atmosphere, paving the way for land animals.

events.desc_zh: 最早的無翅昆蟲在泥盆紀出現，後來演化出翅膀，成為地球上物種最多樣的動物類群。
events.desc_en: The earliest wingless insects appear in the Devonian; they later evolve wings and become Earth's most species-diverse animal group.

events.desc_zh: 魚石螈等早期四足動物從海洋爬上陸地，是所有陸地脊椎動物（包括人類）的祖先。
events.desc_en: Early tetrapods like Tiktaalik crawl onto land — ancestors of all land vertebrates including humans.

events.desc_zh: 具有羊膜卵的爬蟲類演化出現，不再依賴水繁殖，得以征服乾燥的內陸環境。
events.desc_en: Reptiles with amniotic eggs evolve, no longer needing water to reproduce, allowing conquest of dry inland environments.

events.desc_zh: 三疊紀晚期，最早的哺乳類從獸孔目爬蟲演化而來，體型微小，夜行性，恆溫。
events.desc_en: Early mammals evolve from therapsid reptiles in the Late Triassic — tiny, nocturnal, warm-blooded.

events.desc_zh: 侏羅紀晚期的始祖鳥是恐龍與鳥類之間的過渡物種，現代鳥類是恐龍的直系後裔。
events.desc_en: Archaeopteryx in the Late Jurassic is the transitional species between dinosaurs and birds; modern birds are direct dinosaur descendants.

events.desc_zh: 被子植物在白堊紀爆發性演化，與昆蟲共同演化，形成地球上最繁盛的植物類群。
events.desc_en: Angiosperms explode in diversity in the Cretaceous, co-evolving with insects to become Earth's dominant plant group.

events.desc_zh: K-Pg滅絕事件後哺乳類大輻射，最早的靈長類在熱帶森林中演化，具立體視覺與靈活雙手。
events.desc_en: After K-Pg extinction, mammals diversify; earliest primates evolve in tropical forests with stereoscopic vision and grasping hands.

events.en: Apes Split from Old World Monkeys
events.desc_zh: 類人猿與舊世界猴在漸新世末期分化，類人猿支系最終演化出長臂猿、猩猩、黑猩猩與人類。
events.desc_en: Apes diverge from Old World monkeys in the late Oligocene; the ape lineage eventually produces gibbons, orangutans, chimps, and humans.

events.desc_zh: 蘇美人發明人類最早的書寫系統，以蘆葦筆刻在泥板上，用於記錄商業交易與行政管理。
events.desc_en: The Sumerians invent humanity's earliest writing system, pressing reed styluses into clay tablets to record trade and administration.

events.desc_zh: 烏魯克、烏爾、拉格什等城邦興起，建立神廟、王宮與市場，奠定城市文明的基礎。
events.desc_en: City-states like Uruk, Ur, and Lagash rise, building temples, palaces, and markets — the foundation of urban civilization.

events.desc_zh: 薩爾貢一世建立史上第一個帝國，統一兩河流域，開創中央集權帝國的先例。
events.desc_en: Sargon of Akkad builds history's first empire, unifying Mesopotamia and establishing the precedent for centralized imperial rule.

events.desc_zh: 蘇美文明最後的輝煌，以精緻的行政制度與藝術成就著稱，烏爾大神廟在此時期建成。
events.desc_en: The final flourishing of Sumerian civilization, known for sophisticated administration and arts; the Great Ziggurat of Ur built in this period.

events.desc_zh: 猶太教、基督教、伊斯蘭教共同的先祖，一神信仰的起點，從美索不達米亞出發前往迦南。
events.desc_en: The common patriarch of Judaism, Christianity, and Islam; the origin of monotheism, journeying from Mesopotamia to Canaan.

events.desc_zh: 希伯來人在埃及受奴役，為法老建造城市，為出埃及記埋下伏筆。
events.desc_en: The Hebrew people are enslaved in Egypt, building cities for Pharaoh; the prelude to the Exodus.

events.desc_zh: 摩西帶領以色列人走出埃及，十災、過紅海，在西奈山接受十誡，猶太教的奠基事件。
events.desc_en: Moses leads the Israelites out of Egypt through the Ten Plagues and Red Sea, receiving the Ten Commandments at Sinai — the founding event of Judaism.

events.desc_zh: 摩西在西奈山上接受上帝頒布的十誡，成為猶太教、基督教倫理與法律的基礎。
events.desc_en: Moses receives the Ten Commandments on Mount Sinai — the ethical and legal foundation of Judaism and Christianity.

events.desc_zh: 腓尼基人發明22個輔音字母，是幾乎所有現代字母文字（希臘、拉丁、阿拉伯、希伯來）的共同祖先。
events.desc_en: The Phoenicians devise 22 consonant letters — the common ancestor of nearly all modern alphabets: Greek, Latin, Arabic, Hebrew.

events.desc_zh: 大衛王統一以色列王國，定都耶路撒冷，奠定猶太民族的政治與宗教核心，所羅門聖殿在其子任內建成。
events.desc_en: King David unifies Israel, establishes Jerusalem as capital; his son Solomon later builds the First Temple.

events.desc_zh: 第一聖殿在耶路撒冷建成，成為猶太信仰的核心，存放約櫃，586年被巴比倫人摧毀。
events.desc_en: The First Temple in Jerusalem houses the Ark of the Covenant; destroyed by Babylon in 586 BCE.

events.desc_zh: 史上第一個大規模軍事帝國，以殘酷的軍事手段征服中東，建立尼尼微等宏偉城市與圖書館。
events.desc_en: History's first large-scale military empire; conquers the Middle East by force; builds Nineveh and its famous library.

events.desc_zh: 尼布甲尼撒二世滅南國猶大，摧毀聖殿，將猶太人擄至巴比倫，猶太教在流亡中深化。
events.desc_en: Nebuchadnezzar II destroys Jerusalem and the Temple, deporting Jews to Babylon; Judaism deepens during exile.

events.desc_zh: 尼布甲尼撒二世重建巴比倫城，傳說中的空中花園，是古代世界七大奇蹟之一。
events.desc_en: Nebuchadnezzar II rebuilds Babylon; the legendary Hanging Gardens are one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World.

events.desc_zh: 建立波斯阿契美尼德帝國，征服巴比倫後解放被囚猶太人，頒布人類史上最早的人權宣言「居魯士圓柱」。
events.desc_en: Founds the Persian Achaemenid Empire; after conquering Babylon, frees the captive Jews and issues the Cyrus Cylinder — history's earliest human rights declaration.

events.desc_zh: 從希臘到印度的龐大帝國，大流士一世建立行省制度、波斯波利斯宮殿，是古代世界最大帝國之一。
events.desc_en: A vast empire from Greece to India; Darius I establishes the satrapy system and builds Persepolis — one of antiquity's greatest empires.

events.zh: 大流士一世・波斯波利斯
events.desc_zh: 大流士一世鞏固波斯帝國，建造壯麗的波斯波利斯宮殿群，完善行省制度，使帝國達到極盛。
events.desc_en: Darius I consolidates Persian power, builds the magnificent Persepolis palace complex, perfects the satrapy system.

events.desc_zh: 伊朗最後一個前伊斯蘭王朝，瑣羅亞斯德教為國教，與羅馬拜占庭爭雄四百年，最終亡於伊斯蘭軍隊。
events.desc_en: Iran's last pre-Islamic dynasty; Zoroastrianism as state religion; rivaled Rome and Byzantium for 400 years before falling to Islam.

events.desc_zh: 伊斯蘭教先知穆罕默德在麥加出生，其一生的傳道事業將徹底改變中東乃至世界的歷史走向。
events.desc_en: The Prophet Muhammad is born in Mecca; his life's mission would transform the history of the Middle East and the world.

events.desc_zh: 穆罕默德在希拉山洞中接受天使吉卜利勒的啟示，古蘭經第一章降示，伊斯蘭教正式起源。
events.desc_en: Muhammad receives the first revelation from angel Jibreel in the Cave of Hira; the first verse of the Quran is revealed — Islam officially begins.

events.desc_zh: 穆罕默德率軍幾乎不流血地進入麥加，摧毀偶像，使伊斯蘭教成為阿拉伯半島的主導信仰。
events.desc_en: Muhammad's forces enter Mecca nearly bloodlessly, destroy idols, and establish Islam as the dominant faith of the Arabian Peninsula.

events.desc_zh: 第三任哈里發奧斯曼統一古蘭經版本，確立伊斯蘭教的核心經典，成為全球十億穆斯林的精神指引。
events.desc_en: Caliph Uthman standardizes the Quran text, establishing Islam's central scripture — the spiritual guide for over a billion Muslims.

events.desc_zh: 穆罕默德逝後四位繼承者統治，伊斯蘭迅速擴張至波斯、敘利亞、埃及，奠定伊斯蘭世界基礎。
events.desc_en: Four successors after Muhammad; Islam rapidly expands to Persia, Syria, and Egypt, laying the foundation of the Islamic world.

events.desc_zh: 伊斯蘭軍隊擊敗薩珊帝國，波斯文明融入伊斯蘭世界，波斯語、藝術與文化深刻影響伊斯蘭文明。
events.desc_en: Islamic forces defeat the Sassanid Empire; Persian civilization merges with the Islamic world, deeply influencing its culture and arts.

events.desc_zh: 先知之孫胡笙在卡爾巴拉被殺，此事件成為什葉派伊斯蘭的精神核心，遜尼什葉分裂永久化。
events.desc_en: The Prophet's grandson Husayn is killed at Karbala — this becomes the spiritual heart of Shia Islam and permanently deepens the Sunni-Shia split.

events.desc_zh: 伊斯蘭帝國第一個世襲王朝，版圖從西班牙延伸至中亞，首都大馬士革，是當時世界最大國家。
events.desc_en: Islam's first hereditary dynasty; territory stretches from Spain to Central Asia; capital Damascus; one of history's largest states.

events.desc_zh: 伊斯蘭軍隊越過直布羅陀海峽，在數年內征服伊比利亞半島，安達盧斯文明隨之誕生。
events.desc_en: Islamic forces cross the Strait of Gibraltar and conquer the Iberian Peninsula within years, giving birth to the Andalusian civilization.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/伊比利亞半島的穆斯林統治

events.desc_zh: 巴格達成為世界知識中心，阿拉伯學者翻譯保存古希臘典籍，代數、天文、醫學大幅進步。
events.desc_en: Baghdad becomes the world's center of knowledge; Arab scholars translate Greek texts, making great advances in algebra, astronomy, and medicine.

events.desc_zh: 波斯數學家，著《代數學》開創代數這門學科，「Algorithm（演算法）」一詞即源自其名字拉丁化。
events.desc_en: Persian mathematician; his book Al-Kitab al-mukhtasar creates algebra as a discipline; 'Algorithm' derives from the Latin form of his name.

events.desc_zh: 阿拉伯學者，著《光學之書》，首次用實驗方法研究光的折射反射，被譽為現代科學方法的先驅。
events.desc_en: Arab scholar; his Book of Optics studies light with experiment for the first time — considered a pioneer of the scientific method.

events.zh: 伊本・西拿・醫學典範
events.desc_zh: 波斯醫學家，著《醫學典範》，整合古希臘與伊斯蘭醫學知識，在歐洲被使用超過600年。
events.desc_en: Persian physician; his Canon of Medicine synthesizes Greek and Islamic medical knowledge; used in Europe for over 600 years.

events.desc_zh: 庫德族將領薩拉丁統一伊斯蘭世界，從十字軍手中收復耶路撒冷，成為伊斯蘭世界的英雄人物。
events.desc_en: Kurdish leader Saladin unifies the Islamic world and recaptures Jerusalem from the Crusaders — a hero of the Islamic world.

events.zh: 蒙古滅阿拔斯・巴格達陷落
events.desc_zh: 蒙古旭烈兀汗攻陷巴格達，末代哈里發被殺，圖書館典籍投入底格里斯河，伊斯蘭黃金時代終結。
events.desc_en: Mongol Hulagu Khan sacks Baghdad; the last caliph is killed; library books thrown into the Tigris — the Islamic Golden Age ends.

events.desc_zh: 橫跨歐亞非三大洲、延續六百年的伊斯蘭帝國，控制麥加麥地那，主導中東政治至一戰後才瓦解。
events.desc_en: A 600-year Islamic empire spanning three continents; controls Mecca and Medina; dominates Middle Eastern politics until after WWI.

events.zh: 鄂圖曼滅拜占庭・君士坦丁堡陷落
events.desc_zh: 穆罕默德二世攻陷君士坦丁堡，東羅馬帝國千年歷史終結，中世紀結束的象徵性事件。
events.desc_en: Mehmed II conquers Constantinople; the 1,000-year Eastern Roman Empire ends — a symbolic close of the Middle Ages.

events.desc_zh: 凱末爾廢除鄂圖曼哈里發制度，伊斯蘭世界失去統一的宗教政治領袖，現代世俗土耳其建立。
events.desc_en: Atatürk abolishes the Ottoman caliphate; the Islamic world loses its unified religious-political leader; modern secular Turkey established.

events.desc_zh: 二戰後猶太人在巴勒斯坦建立以色列國，引發第一次中東戰爭，巴以衝突延續至今。
events.desc_en: After WWII, Jews establish the State of Israel in Palestine, triggering the first Arab-Israeli War; the conflict continues to the present.

events.desc_zh: 何梅尼領導的伊斯蘭革命推翻美國支持的巴勒維王朝，建立政教合一的伊斯蘭共和國，美伊關係決裂。伊朗從此成為中東最重要的什葉派力量，與沙烏地阿拉伯（遜尼派）的教派對抗深刻影響中東格局。
events.desc_en: Khomeini leads the overthrow of the Shah, establishing an Islamic Republic — a pivotal symbol of political Islam reshaping the Middle East.

events.desc_zh: 希臘數學家與哲學家，畢達哥拉斯定理、數字的神秘性與音律的數學關係，影響西方思想深遠。
events.desc_en: Greek mathematician and philosopher; the Pythagorean theorem, the mysticism of numbers, and the mathematics of musical harmony.

events.zh: 希波克拉底・西方醫學
events.desc_zh: 西方醫學之父，將醫學從迷信提升為理性學科，希波克拉底誓詞至今仍是醫學倫理的基礎。
events.desc_en: Father of Western medicine; elevated medicine from superstition to rational discipline; the Hippocratic Oath remains medicine's ethical foundation.

events.desc_zh: 西方哲學最重要的奠基者之一，創立學院（Academia），理念論影響西方思想兩千年。
events.desc_en: One of the most important founders of Western philosophy; established the Academy; the Theory of Forms influenced Western thought for two millennia.

events.desc_zh: 百科全書式學者，建立邏輯學、生物學、政治學、倫理學等學科體系，是古代最有影響力的思想家。
events.desc_en: The encyclopedic scholar who established logic, biology, politics, and ethics as disciplines — antiquity's most influential thinker.

events.desc_zh: 《幾何原本》系統化了幾何學知識，以公理演繹體系影響西方數學兩千年，是史上最具影響力的數學著作。
events.desc_en: Elements systematizes geometry with an axiomatic deductive system — the most influential mathematical work in history, used for 2,000 years.

events.desc_zh: 古代最偉大的數學家與工程師，發現浮力原理、計算圓周率、發明螺旋泵，奠定靜力學基礎。
events.desc_en: The greatest mathematician and engineer of antiquity; discovered buoyancy, calculated pi, invented the Archimedean screw, founded statics.

events.desc_zh: 奧古斯都稱帝開啟帝國時代，羅馬法律、建築、語言（拉丁文）奠定西方文明的基礎，影響延續至今。
events.desc_en: Augustus becomes emperor; Roman law, architecture, and Latin language lay the foundations of Western civilization — their influence endures today.

events.desc_zh: 維蘇威火山爆發埋葬龐貝城，意外保存了羅馬帝國時期完整的城市面貌，成為最重要的考古遺址之一。
events.desc_en: Vesuvius buries Pompeii, accidentally preserving a complete Roman city — now one of the most important archaeological sites in the world.

events.zh: 米蘭詔書・基督教合法化
events.desc_zh: 君士坦丁大帝頒布米蘭詔書，基督教在羅馬帝國合法化，為後來成為國教奠定基礎。
events.desc_en: Emperor Constantine issues the Edict of Milan, legalizing Christianity in the Roman Empire — the foundation for it becoming the state religion.

events.desc_zh: 狄奧多西一世逝後，羅馬帝國永久分裂為東西兩部，西羅馬80年後滅亡，東羅馬（拜占庭）延續千年。
events.desc_en: After Theodosius I dies, the Empire permanently splits; the Western Empire falls 80 years later; the Eastern Empire (Byzantium) survives for 1,000 years.

events.desc_zh: 東羅馬帝國在君士坦丁堡延續千年，保存古希臘羅馬文化，是連接古典與中世紀歐洲的橋樑。
events.desc_en: The Eastern Roman Empire survives for 1,000 years in Constantinople, preserving Greco-Roman culture and bridging classical antiquity to medieval Europe.

events.desc_zh: 東羅馬最偉大的皇帝，編纂《查士丁尼法典》奠定歐洲法律基礎，建造聖索菲亞大教堂，短暫重建羅馬帝國版圖。
events.desc_en: Greatest Byzantine emperor; compiles Justinian's Code laying foundations of European law; builds Hagia Sophia; briefly restores Roman territory.

events.desc_zh: 耶穌在耶路撒冷被釘十字架，三天後復活（據信），此事件成為基督教信仰的核心與起點。
events.desc_en: Jesus is crucified in Jerusalem and resurrected three days later (according to Christian belief) — the core and founding event of Christianity.

events.desc_zh: 使徒保羅展開三次大規模傳教旅程，將基督教從猶太社群推廣至希臘羅馬世界，奠定教會基礎。
events.desc_en: Apostle Paul undertakes three major missionary journeys, spreading Christianity from Jewish communities to the Greco-Roman world.

events.desc_zh: 第一次基督教大公會議，確立三位一體教義，確定復活節日期，奠定正統基督教教義的基礎。
events.desc_en: The first ecumenical council establishes the Trinity doctrine and fixes Easter — laying the foundation of orthodox Christian theology.

events.desc_zh: 狄奧多西一世頒布詔令，基督教成為羅馬帝國唯一合法宗教，異教遭到禁止，歐洲基督教化開始。
events.desc_en: Theodosius I decrees Christianity the only legal religion in the Roman Empire; paganism banned; the Christianization of Europe begins.

events.desc_zh: 羅馬天主教與君士坦丁堡東正教永久分裂，互相革除教籍，基督教世界一分為二，影響延續至今。
events.desc_en: The Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox churches permanently split, excommunicating each other — Christianity divided in two, with lasting consequences.

events.desc_zh: 喀爾文在日內瓦建立神權政治，預定論思想影響清教徒革命、美國清教徒移民與資本主義發展。
events.desc_en: Calvin establishes a theocracy in Geneva; his doctrine of predestination influences Puritan revolution, American Puritans, and capitalism's development.

events.desc_zh: 依納爵・羅耀拉創立耶穌會，作為反宗教改革的精銳力量，將基督教傳播至亞洲、非洲與美洲。
events.desc_en: Ignatius of Loyola founds the Jesuits as the vanguard of the Counter-Reformation, spreading Christianity to Asia, Africa, and the Americas.

events.desc_zh: 亨利八世因離婚問題與羅馬教廷決裂，建立英格蘭國教（聖公會），此後英國宗教改革延續數十年。
events.desc_en: Henry VIII breaks with Rome over his divorce, founding the Church of England; the English Reformation continues for decades.

events.desc_zh: 天主教現代化改革的里程碑，以拉丁語以外的語言舉行彌撒，與其他宗教展開對話，影響全球十億天主教徒。
events.desc_en: Landmark Catholic modernization: Mass celebrated in vernacular languages; dialogue with other religions; affects one billion Catholics worldwide.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/第二次梵蒂岡大公會議

events.desc_zh: 查理曼統一西歐大部分地區，800年加冕為神聖羅馬皇帝，被稱為「歐洲之父」，奠定現代歐洲雛形。
events.desc_en: Charlemagne unifies most of Western Europe; crowned Holy Roman Emperor in 800; called 'Father of Europe' — the prototype of modern Europe.

events.desc_zh: 中世紀歐洲的核心政治結構，由德意志諸侯與義大利城邦組成，伏爾泰諷刺其「既不神聖，也不羅馬，更非帝國」。
events.desc_en: The core political structure of medieval Europe; Voltaire quipped it was 'neither holy, nor Roman, nor an empire.'

events.desc_zh: 諾曼第公爵威廉在黑斯廷斯戰役擊敗英王哈羅德，奠定英國王室與貴族制度，使英語融入大量法語詞彙。
events.desc_en: William of Normandy defeats King Harold at Hastings, founding the English royal dynasty and infusing English with vast French vocabulary.

events.desc_zh: 英國貴族迫使約翰王簽署，限制王權、保障貴族權利，是現代憲政與法治精神的最早文件之一。
events.desc_en: English barons force King John to sign, limiting royal power — one of the earliest documents of constitutionalism and rule of law.

events.desc_zh: 英法兩國為爭奪法國王位爆發的長期戰爭，聖女貞德扭轉戰局，最終促成法國民族意識的覺醒。
events.desc_en: A prolonged war for the French throne; Joan of Arc turns the tide; ultimately awakens French national identity.

events.zh: 伽利略・望遠鏡天文學
events.desc_zh: 伽利略改良望遠鏡並指向天空，發現木星衛星、金星相位，以實驗方法捍衛哥白尼日心說，奠定近代科學基礎。
events.desc_en: Galileo improves the telescope and turns it skyward; discovers Jupiter's moons and Venus phases; defends heliocentrism with experiment, founding modern science.

events.desc_zh: 克卜勒發現行星以橢圓軌道繞太陽運行的三大定律，是牛頓萬有引力定律的重要前提。
events.desc_en: Kepler discovers the three laws of planetary motion — planets orbit in ellipses; the crucial foundation for Newton's law of gravitation.

events.desc_zh: 威廉・哈維發表《心血運動論》，正確描述血液循環系統，推翻蓋倫的錯誤理論，開創現代生理學。
events.desc_en: William Harvey publishes on the circulation of blood, correctly describing the cardiovascular system and overturning Galen's errors — founding modern physiology.

events.desc_zh: 歐洲最慘烈的宗教戰爭，造成中歐人口銳減三分之一，威斯特伐利亞和約確立現代主權國家體系。
events.desc_en: Europe's most devastating religious war; Central Europe loses a third of its population; the Peace of Westphalia establishes the modern sovereign state system.

events.desc_zh: 不流血的政變推翻詹姆士二世，確立議會主權，《權利法案》奠定君主立憲制度，深刻影響美國憲法。
events.desc_en: A bloodless coup overthrows James II; parliamentary sovereignty confirmed; the Bill of Rights establishes constitutional monarchy, influencing the US Constitution.

events.desc_zh: 安托萬・拉瓦節命名氧氣、氫氣，推翻燃素說，建立質量守恆定律，奠定現代化學的基礎。
events.desc_en: Antoine Lavoisier names oxygen and hydrogen, disproves phlogiston theory, establishes the law of conservation of mass — founding modern chemistry.

events.desc_zh: 拿破崙從法國大革命中崛起，征服歐洲大部，拿破崙法典影響全球法律體系，最終在滑鐵盧落敗。
events.desc_en: Napoleon rises from the Revolution, conquers much of Europe; the Napoleonic Code reshapes global law; finally defeated at Waterloo.

events.desc_zh: 法拉第發現電磁感應定律，馬克士威統一電磁理論，奠定電力、無線通訊與現代技術文明的基礎。
events.desc_en: Faraday discovers electromagnetic induction; Maxwell unifies electromagnetic theory — the foundation of electrical power and modern communications.

events.desc_zh: 俄國化學家門得列夫發表元素週期表，預測未知元素的存在，是化學史上最重要的分類系統。
events.desc_en: Russian chemist Mendeleev publishes the periodic table, predicting the existence of undiscovered elements — the most important classification system in chemistry.

events.desc_zh: 俾斯麥以「鐵血政策」統一德意志各邦，建立德意志帝國，改變歐洲權力格局，埋下一戰伏筆。
events.desc_en: Bismarck unifies German states through 'blood and iron,' establishing the German Empire — reshaping European power and sowing seeds of WWI.

events.desc_zh: 文藝復興全才，《蒙娜麗莎》《最後的晚餐》作者，同時研究解剖學、飛行器、水力機械，超越時代的天才。
events.desc_en: Renaissance polymath; painter of the Mona Lisa and Last Supper; simultaneously studied anatomy, flying machines, and hydraulics — a genius centuries ahead of his time.

events.desc_zh: 費爾南多・麥哲倫率領的船隊完成人類首次環球航行，實際證明地球是球形的，徹底改變世界觀。
events.desc_en: Ferdinand Magellan's expedition completes the first circumnavigation of Earth, proving beyond doubt that the world is round.

events.desc_zh: 希特勒掌權建立極權政府，發動二戰造成五千萬人死亡，猶太大屠殺是人類史上最黑暗的罪行之一。
events.desc_en: Hitler seizes power, building a totalitarian state; WWII kills 50 million; the Holocaust is one of history's darkest crimes.

events.zh: 歐洲一體化・羅馬條約
events.desc_zh: 法德等六國簽署羅馬條約，建立歐洲經濟共同體，歐盟的前身，開創主權國家自願整合的先例。
events.desc_en: Six nations sign the Treaty of Rome, creating the European Economic Community — the EU's predecessor and a historic experiment in voluntary sovereignty integration.

events.desc_zh: 東德開放邊境，柏林圍牆被推倒，冷戰時代象徵性終結，德國統一與蘇聯解體相繼而來。
events.desc_en: East Germany opens its borders; the Berlin Wall falls — the symbolic end of the Cold War, followed by German reunification and the Soviet collapse.

events.desc_zh: 蘇聯正式解體，冷戰終結，15個獨立國家誕生，美國成為唯一超級大國，世界進入單極時代。
events.desc_en: The USSR officially dissolves; the Cold War ends; 15 independent states emerge; the US becomes the sole superpower; the world enters a unipolar era.

events.desc_zh: 愛因斯坦發表狹義相對論，E=mc²改變人類對時間、空間與能量的理解，奠定核能與現代宇宙學基礎。
events.desc_en: Einstein publishes special relativity; E=mc² transforms our understanding of time, space, and energy — the foundation of nuclear energy and modern cosmology.

events.desc_zh: 波耳、海森堡、薛丁格等人建立量子力學，揭示微觀世界的不確定性，是半導體、雷射與核技術的理論基礎。
events.desc_en: Bohr, Heisenberg, Schrödinger establish quantum mechanics, revealing the uncertainty of the microscopic world — the theoretical basis of semiconductors, lasers, and nuclear technology.

events.desc_zh: 中國最重要的古代數學著作，包含聯立方程、負數、分數運算，系統地解決農業與工程問題。
events.desc_en: China's most important ancient math text; covers simultaneous equations, negative numbers, and fractions — systematically solving agricultural and engineering problems.

events.desc_zh: 中國人發明養蠶繅絲技術，絲綢成為中國最重要的出口商品，也是絲路命名的由來。
events.desc_en: Chinese invent silk cultivation; silk becomes China's most important export and gives the Silk Road its name.

events.desc_zh: 李冰父子在四川建造的水利工程，無壩引水，造就天府之國，至今仍在運作，是世界最古老的水利工程之一。
events.desc_en: Built by Li Bing in Sichuan; a damless irrigation system still in use today — one of the world's oldest and most sustainable water projects.

events.desc_zh: 漢代發展出高爐煉鐵技術，大規模生產鐵農具與武器，領先歐洲約千年，支撐帝國的農業與軍事擴張。
events.desc_en: Han Dynasty develops blast furnace iron production on a large scale — about 1,000 years ahead of Europe — supporting imperial agriculture and military expansion.

events.desc_zh: 蔡倫改良造紙技術，以樹皮、麻料等製成廉價紙張，知識傳播的革命性突破，是中國四大發明之一。
events.desc_en: Cai Lun improves papermaking using bark and hemp; cheap paper revolutionizes knowledge transmission — one of China's Four Great Inventions.

events.desc_zh: 張衡發明世界第一台地震儀，以銅球落入蟾蜍口中指示地震方向，比歐洲同類裝置早1700年。
events.desc_en: Zhang Heng invents the world's first seismoscope; a bronze ball drops into a toad's mouth to indicate earthquake direction — 1,700 years before European equivalents.

events.desc_zh: 張衡製作水力驅動的渾天儀，精確測量天體位置，推算太陽月球運行，對中國天文學貢獻卓著。
events.desc_en: Zhang Heng builds a water-powered armillary sphere to precisely measure celestial positions and predict the movements of the sun and moon.

events.desc_zh: 東漢神醫華佗使用麻沸散（全身麻醉藥）進行外科手術，是世界最早有記錄的外科麻醉應用。
events.desc_en: Han physician Hua Tuo uses mafeisan (general anesthetic) for surgery — the world's earliest recorded use of surgical anesthesia.

events.desc_zh: 劉徽以割圓術計算圓周率，得π≈3.14159，方法上已含無窮小的極限概念，是數學史上的重要成就。
events.desc_en: Liu Hui calculates π≈3.14159 using an iterative polygon method — embedding the concept of limits, a major achievement in mathematical history.

events.desc_zh: 中醫學的理論基礎，以陰陽五行解釋人體與疾病，至今仍是中醫教育的核心典籍。
events.desc_en: The theoretical foundation of Chinese medicine; explains the body and disease through yin-yang and five elements — still a core text in TCM education.

events.desc_zh: 宋代中國人發明人痘接種預防天花，是現代疫苗接種的前身，17世紀傳入歐洲後推動了天花疫苗的研發。
events.desc_en: Song Dynasty Chinese develop variolation against smallpox — the predecessor of modern vaccination; introduced to Europe in the 17th century, inspiring Jenner's vaccine.

events.desc_zh: 畢昇發明陶瓷活字印刷術，比古騰堡的活字印刷機早400年，是知識傳播史上的重大技術革命。
events.desc_en: Bi Sheng invents ceramic movable type — 400 years before Gutenberg — a major technological revolution in the history of knowledge dissemination.

events.desc_zh: 中國人將指南針從堪輿術應用於航海導航，此技術傳入歐洲後直接促進大航海時代的到來。
events.desc_en: Chinese apply the compass from feng shui to maritime navigation; this technology reaches Europe and directly enables the Age of Exploration.

events.desc_zh: 楊輝發表二項式係數三角形，比歐洲帕斯卡三角形早400年，應用於多項式展開與概率計算。
events.desc_en: Yang Hui publishes the binomial coefficient triangle — 400 years before Pascal's Triangle in Europe — applied to polynomial expansion and probability.

events.en: Zu Chongzhi — Pi to 7 Decimal Places
events.desc_zh: 祖沖之計算圓周率精確到小數點後7位（3.1415926），此記錄保持了約千年，至今仍是數學史的重要成就。
events.desc_en: Zu Chongzhi calculates π to 7 decimal places (3.1415926) — a world record held for ~1,000 years and a landmark in mathematical history.

events.desc_zh: 唐代道士煉丹時意外發現火藥，後來廣泛應用於軍事，傳入歐洲後改變了中世紀的戰爭型態。
events.desc_en: Tang Dynasty Taoist alchemists accidentally discover gunpowder; it later transforms warfare and, reaching Europe, changes medieval combat forever.

events.desc_zh: 隋煬帝開鑿全長2700公里的大運河，連接南北方，促進糧食與商品流通，是世界最長的人工河道。
events.desc_en: Emperor Yang of Sui builds the 2,700km Grand Canal connecting north and south China — the world's longest artificial waterway, facilitating grain and trade.

events.desc_zh: 唐代高僧僧一行主持世界首次大規模大地測量，計算出地球子午線弧度，是科學史的重要里程碑。
events.desc_en: Tang monk Yi Xing leads the world's first large-scale geodetic survey, measuring the arc of a meridian — a landmark in scientific history.

events.desc_zh: 元代郭守敬制定授時曆，一年長度精確到365.2425天，與今日格里曆完全相同，精度超越當時歐洲任何曆法。
events.desc_en: Yuan astronomer Guo Shoujing's Shoushi Calendar sets the year at 365.2425 days — identical to the modern Gregorian calendar — more accurate than any contemporary European calendar.

events.en: Li Shizhen — Compendium of Materia Medica
events.desc_zh: 李時珍耗時27年完成《本草綱目》，收錄1892種藥物，被譯成多種語言，是東方藥學的百科全書。
events.desc_en: Li Shizhen spends 27 years writing this encyclopedia of 1,892 medicinal substances — translated into many languages; the definitive compendium of Eastern pharmacology.

events.desc_zh: 明代地理學家徐霞客實地考察中國山川地貌，著《徐霞客遊記》，是中國近代地理學的先驅。
events.desc_en: Ming geographer Xu Xiake surveys China's landscape on foot; his travel notes are a pioneering work of Chinese empirical geography.

events.desc_zh: 鄭和率領當時世界最大的船隊七下西洋，到達非洲東岸，展現中國的航海實力，早於歐洲大航海時代半世紀。
events.desc_en: Zheng He leads the world's largest fleet on seven voyages reaching East Africa — demonstrating China's maritime power half a century before Europe's Age of Exploration.

events.desc_zh: 太陽系第四行星，有古老河床與極冠，是最可能存在外星生命痕跡的行星。
events.desc_en: The fourth planet; ancient riverbeds and polar ice caps make it the most likely candidate for extraterrestrial life.

events.desc_zh: 與地球大小相近的孿生行星，但極端溫室效應使表面溫度高達465度，是太陽系最熱的行星。
events.desc_en: Earth's twin in size but extreme greenhouse effect makes its surface 465°C — the hottest planet in the solar system.

events.desc_zh: 自轉軸傾斜98度的冰態巨行星，幾乎是「躺著」繞太陽公轉，擁有13條環和27顆衛星。
events.desc_en: Ice giant with a 98° axial tilt, essentially orbiting on its side; has 13 rings and 27 moons.

events.desc_zh: 太陽系最外層的大行星，風速高達2100公里/小時，擁有太陽系最強烈的風暴。
events.desc_en: The outermost major planet; wind speeds up to 2,100 km/h — the most violent storms in the solar system.

events.desc_zh: 元古宙最早的時代，大氧化事件改變大氣成分，休倫冰河期使地球幾乎全部凍結，生命以原核生物為主。
events.desc_en: Earliest era of the Proterozoic; the Great Oxidation Event transforms the atmosphere; Huronian glaciation nearly freezes the Earth.

events.desc_zh: 生命開始出現有性生殖，多細胞藻類演化，羅迪尼亞超大陸形成，海洋生態系逐漸複雜化。
events.desc_en: Sexual reproduction evolves; multicellular algae appear; Rodinia supercontinent assembles; marine ecosystems grow more complex.

events.desc_zh: 雪球地球多次凍結，埃迪卡拉紀最早複雜動物出現，為寒武紀大爆發奠基。
events.desc_en: Multiple Snowball Earth events; earliest complex animals appear in the Ediacaran; groundwork laid for the Cambrian explosion.

events.desc_zh: 有性生殖的演化使基因可以重組，大幅加速物種的演化速度與多樣性，是生命史的關鍵創新。
events.desc_en: The evolution of sexual reproduction allows genetic recombination, dramatically accelerating evolutionary speed and diversity.

events.desc_zh: 最早可以肉眼看見的多細胞藻類出現，光合作用效率大幅提升，為海洋食物鏈奠基。
events.desc_en: The first visible multicellular algae appear; photosynthetic efficiency increases dramatically; the foundation of marine food chains.

events.desc_zh: 地球兩次幾乎完全凍結（雪球地球），海洋表面結冰，生命在海底熱液口存活，結束後生命爆發性複雜化。
events.desc_en: Earth freezes twice (Snowball Earth); life survives near hydrothermal vents; after thawing, life complexifies explosively.

events.desc_zh: 海綿是已知最早的動物，無神經系統，以過濾海水為食，是動物界所有物種的最遠祖先。
events.desc_en: Sponges are the earliest known animals — no nervous system, filter-feeding; the most distant ancestor of all animal species.

events.desc_zh: 老子著《道德經》，創立道家思想，「道」的哲學影響中國文化兩千年，後來演變為道教信仰。
events.desc_en: Laozi writes the Tao Te Ching, founding Taoist philosophy; the concept of the Tao shapes Chinese culture for 2,000 years, later evolving into religious Taoism.

events.desc_zh: 張道陵創立天師道，道教從哲學思想正式發展為有組織的宗教，設立科儀、經典與教派制度。
events.desc_en: Zhang Daoling founds the Way of the Celestial Masters; Taoism formally develops from philosophy into an organized religion with rituals and canon.

events.desc_zh: 王重陽創立全真道，融合儒釋道三家，強調內丹修煉與道德修養，是北方道教最重要的支派。
events.desc_en: Wang Chongyang founds the Quanzhen school, integrating Confucian, Buddhist, and Taoist elements; the most important northern Taoist sect.

events.desc_zh: 《吠陀》四部聖典在此期間形成，印度教的神學基礎、種姓制度與祭祀傳統均源於此時期。
events.desc_en: The four Vedas take shape during this period; the theological foundation of Hinduism, the caste system, and sacrificial traditions all originate here.

events.desc_zh: 印度哲學最深刻的思想著作，探討梵我合一、業力輪迴、解脫之道，奠定印度哲學的核心概念。
events.desc_en: India's most profound philosophical texts; explore Brahman-Atman unity, karma, reincarnation, and liberation — the core concepts of Indian philosophy.

events.desc_zh: 印度教最重要的經典之一，記載克里希納對阿周那的教導，討論責任、靈魂、虔誠之道，影響深遠。
events.desc_en: One of Hinduism's most important scriptures; Krishna's teachings to Arjuna on duty, the soul, and devotional paths — profoundly influential worldwide.

events.desc_zh: 孔雀王朝阿育王皈依佛教，派遣傳教士至斯里蘭卡、中亞，使佛教從地區性信仰轉為世界宗教。
events.desc_en: Maurya Emperor Ashoka converts to Buddhism and sends missionaries to Sri Lanka and Central Asia, transforming Buddhism from regional faith to world religion.

events.desc_zh: 東漢明帝時期，白馬寺建立，佛教正式傳入中國，與儒道融合後發展出具有中國特色的禪宗等流派。
events.desc_en: During the Eastern Han, the White Horse Temple is built; Buddhism officially enters China, later merging with Confucianism and Taoism to produce distinctly Chinese schools like Chan (Zen).

events.desc_zh: 菩提達摩傳入禪宗，強調頓悟與冥想，後來傳入日本成為禪宗，深刻影響東亞藝術、武術與哲學。
events.desc_en: Bodhidharma brings Chan Buddhism; emphasis on meditation and sudden enlightenment; spreads to Japan as Zen, profoundly influencing East Asian art and philosophy.

events.zh: 上座部佛教傳入東南亞
events.desc_zh: 上座部佛教傳入緬甸、泰國、柬埔寨、寮國，成為東南亞的主流信仰，影響當地文化藝術至今。
events.desc_en: Theravada Buddhism spreads to Myanmar, Thailand, Cambodia, and Laos, becoming the dominant faith of Southeast Asia and deeply influencing local culture.

events.desc_zh: 猶太教最核心的經典《摩西五書》（創世記至申命記）在此時期定型成文，奠定猶太律法與信仰基礎。
events.desc_en: The Torah (Genesis through Deuteronomy) takes its written form, establishing the foundation of Jewish law and faith.

events.desc_zh: 被擄歸回的猶太人重建耶路撒冷聖殿，第二聖殿時期是猶太教發展的關鍵階段，直至70年被羅馬摧毀。
events.desc_en: Jews returning from Babylon rebuild the Jerusalem Temple; the Second Temple Period is crucial for Jewish development until its destruction by Rome in 70 CE.

events.desc_zh: 羅馬將軍提圖斯摧毀耶路撒冷第二聖殿，猶太人大流散開始，哭牆是唯一留存的遺跡。
events.desc_en: Roman general Titus destroys the Second Temple; the Jewish diaspora begins; the Western Wall is the only surviving remnant.

events.desc_zh: 迦太基公會議確立27卷新約聖經的正典範圍，奠定基督教聖經的最終形式，影響教義發展至今。
events.desc_en: The Council of Carthage establishes the 27-book New Testament canon — the final form of Christian scripture, shaping doctrine to this day.

events.desc_zh: 馬丁路德《九十五條論綱》引發宗教改革，基督新教誕生，天主教一統的局面終結，深刻改變歐洲政治宗教格局。
events.desc_en: Luther's 95 Theses spark the Reformation; Protestantism is born; Catholic unity ends; Europe's political-religious landscape is permanently transformed.

events.desc_zh: 黃河流域的新石器時代文化，以彩陶著稱，已有農業定居聚落，是中國文明的重要史前基礎。
events.desc_en: Neolithic culture of the Yellow River valley, known for painted pottery; agricultural settlements form the prehistoric foundation of Chinese civilization.

events.desc_zh: 三皇之一的神農氏教導人民農耕與醫藥，親嘗百草辨別藥效，是中國農業文明與中醫的傳說起源。
events.desc_en: One of the Three Sovereigns, Shennong taught farming and medicine, tasting hundreds of herbs — the legendary origin of Chinese agriculture and traditional medicine.

events.desc_zh: 黃河流域晚期新石器時代文化，已有城邑、玉器、黑陶，出現早期社會分層，是夏朝的直接前身。
events.desc_en: Late Neolithic culture of the Yellow River valley; walled towns, jade artifacts, and black pottery appear; direct precursor to the Xia Dynasty.

events.en: Yellow Emperor — Origin of Chinese Civilization
events.desc_zh: 黃帝被視為中華民族的始祖，傳說創制文字、曆法、音樂、醫學，黃帝紀元是中國五千年文明的起點。
events.desc_en: The Yellow Emperor is regarded as the ancestor of the Chinese people; legend credits him with creating writing, the calendar, music, and medicine — the start of 5,000 years of Chinese civilization.

events.desc_zh: 希臘青銅時代最重要的文明，特洛伊戰爭的歷史背景，後因「海上民族」入侵而崩潰，進入希臘黑暗時代。
events.desc_en: The dominant Greek Bronze Age civilization; the historical context of the Trojan War; collapsed due to the Sea Peoples invasion, leading to the Greek Dark Ages.

events.desc_zh: 荷馬創作《伊里亞德》和《奧德賽》，奠定西方文學傳統，影響兩千年歐洲文學創作。
events.desc_en: Homer composes the Iliad and Odyssey, founding the Western literary tradition and influencing 2,000 years of European literature.

events.desc_zh: 道德經五千言，是中國哲學與文學的瑰寶，對東亞思想文化影響深遠。
events.desc_en: The 5,000-character Tao Te Ching is a treasure of Chinese philosophy and literature with profound influence on East Asian thought.

events.desc_zh: 論語記載孔子的言行，是儒家文化的核心典籍，影響東亞文明超過兩千年。
events.desc_en: The Analects records Confucius's teachings — the core text of Confucian culture, influencing East Asian civilization for over 2,000 years.

events.desc_zh: 雅典衛城的帕德嫩神廟是古希臘建築的頂峰，多立克柱式的完美典範，影響西方建築兩千年。
events.desc_en: The Parthenon on the Acropolis is the pinnacle of ancient Greek architecture — the perfect Doric exemplar, influencing Western architecture for 2,000 years.

events.en: Li Bai & Du Fu — Tang Poetry
events.desc_zh: 李白與杜甫是唐詩的雙峰，詩仙詩聖的作品代表中國文學最高成就，廣泛影響東亞文化。
events.desc_en: Li Bai and Du Fu are the twin peaks of Tang poetry; their works represent the highest achievement of Chinese literature, widely influencing East Asian culture.

events.desc_zh: 但丁以義大利語寫成的《神曲》開創文藝復興文學先河，被譽為中世紀最偉大的文學作品。
events.desc_en: Dante's Divine Comedy, written in Italian vernacular, pioneers Renaissance literature and is regarded as the greatest work of medieval literature.

events.zh: 米開朗基羅・西斯廷天花板
events.desc_zh: 米開朗基羅歷時四年完成西斯廷禮拜堂天花板壁畫，《創世紀》是文藝復興藝術的巔峰之作。
events.desc_en: Michelangelo spends four years painting the Sistine Chapel ceiling; the Creation of Adam is the pinnacle of Renaissance art.

events.desc_zh: 莎士比亞創作37部戲劇與154首十四行詩，是英語文學最重要的作家，深刻影響全球文學與戲劇。
events.desc_en: Shakespeare writes 37 plays and 154 sonnets — the most important writer in the English language, profoundly influencing world literature and drama.

events.zh: 巴赫・貝多芬（古典音樂）
events.desc_zh: 巴赫奠定複調音樂基礎，貝多芬開創浪漫主義音樂，兩人代表西方古典音樂的最高峰。
events.desc_en: Bach establishes the foundations of polyphonic music; Beethoven pioneers Romanticism — together they represent the pinnacle of Western classical music.

events.desc_zh: 莫內等人發起印象派運動，打破傳統繪畫規範，以光影與色彩直接呈現瞬間感受，開創現代藝術先河。
events.desc_en: Monet and others launch Impressionism, breaking traditional painting rules to capture fleeting moments of light and color — pioneering modern art.

events.desc_zh: 曹雪芹著《紅樓夢》是中國古典小說的巔峰，展現封建大家族的興衰，文學與社會學價值無可比擬。
events.desc_en: Cao Xueqin's Dream of the Red Chamber is the pinnacle of Chinese classical fiction, depicting a noble family's rise and fall with incomparable literary and sociological value.

events.desc_zh: 阿拉伯民間故事集，薛赫拉莎德的故事框架收錄數百個中東、印度、波斯的民間故事，影響全球文學。
events.desc_en: A collection of Middle Eastern folk tales within Scheherazade's framing narrative — hundreds of stories from the Arab, Indian, and Persian traditions, influencing world literature.

events.desc_zh: 蒙兀兒皇帝沙賈汗為亡妻建造的陵墓，是伊斯蘭建築與蒙兀兒藝術的頂峰，世界七大奇蹟之一。
events.desc_en: Built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan for his wife, the Taj Mahal is the pinnacle of Islamic architecture and Mughal art — one of the New Seven Wonders.

events.desc_zh: 畢卡索創立立體主義，徹底打破透視法，開創20世紀現代藝術的革命性浪潮。
events.desc_en: Picasso founds Cubism, completely breaking perspective conventions and launching the revolutionary wave of 20th-century modern art.

events.zh: 人類越過白令陸橋進入美洲
events.desc_zh: 人類在末次冰河期透過白令陸橋從亞洲進入北美洲，逐漸擴散至整個南北美洲，成為美洲原住民的祖先。
events.desc_en: During the last glacial period, humans cross the Bering Land Bridge from Asia into North America, gradually spreading throughout the Americas.

events.desc_zh: 中美洲最早的主要文明，以巨石頭像著稱，對後來的馬雅、阿茲特克文明影響深遠，被稱為「母文化」。
events.desc_en: The earliest major Mesoamerican civilization, known for colossal stone heads; the 'mother culture' that influenced Maya and Aztec civilizations.

events.desc_zh: 中美洲最重要的古代文明，發展出精密曆法、象形文字、天文學，建造壯觀的石造城市。
events.desc_en: The most significant ancient Mesoamerican civilization, developing precise calendars, hieroglyphics, astronomy, and spectacular stone cities.

events.desc_zh: 北美洲各地原住民發展出多元文化，包括密西西比文化的土丘建築、太平洋西北岸的圖騰柱文化等。
events.desc_en: Diverse indigenous cultures across North America, including Mississippian mound builders and Pacific Northwest totem pole cultures.

events.desc_zh: 中美洲強大帝國，以特諾奇提特蘭為首都，發展出複雜的宗教、農業和貿易體系，1521年被西班牙征服者推翻。
events.desc_en: Powerful Mesoamerican empire centered on Tenochtitlan, with complex religion, agriculture, and trade; conquered by Spanish in 1521.

events.desc_zh: 南美洲最大的前哥倫布帝國，沿安地斯山脈延伸，建有精密的道路系統和梯田農業，1533年被西班牙征服者攻滅。
events.desc_en: The largest pre-Columbian empire in the Americas, stretching along the Andes with sophisticated road systems and terraced agriculture; conquered by Spain in 1533.

events.desc_zh: 哥倫布代表西班牙航行抵達加勒比海，開啟歐洲對美洲的殖民時代，改變了世界歷史格局。
events.desc_en: Columbus, sailing for Spain, reaches the Caribbean, opening the era of European colonization of the Americas and transforming world history.

events.desc_zh: 埃爾南·科爾特斯率西班牙軍隊征服阿茲特克帝國，開啟中美洲的西班牙殖民統治。
events.desc_en: Hernán Cortés leads Spanish forces to conquer the Aztec Empire, beginning Spanish colonial rule in Mesoamerica.

events.desc_zh: 北美十三殖民地宣布脫離英國統治，建立美利堅合眾國，是現代民主政治的重要里程碑。
events.desc_en: The thirteen North American colonies declare independence from Britain, establishing the United States — a landmark of modern democracy.

events.desc_zh: 美國憲法正式生效，建立三權分立的聯邦制政府，成為現代憲政民主的典範。
events.desc_en: The US Constitution takes effect, establishing a federal government with separation of powers — a model for modern constitutional democracy.

events.desc_zh: 海地成為世界上第一個由奴隸起義建立的黑人共和國，是拉丁美洲獨立運動的先驅。
events.desc_en: Haiti becomes the world's first Black republic founded by a slave revolt — a pioneer of Latin American independence movements.

events.desc_zh: 玻利瓦爾、聖馬丁等領導拉丁美洲各國脫離西班牙葡萄牙統治，建立一系列獨立共和國。
events.desc_en: Led by Bolívar and San Martín, Latin American nations break free from Spanish and Portuguese rule, establishing independent republics.

events.desc_zh: 美國南北方為奴隸制存廢爆發內戰，北方聯邦獲勝，奴隸制廢除，美國維持統一並走向工業化。
events.desc_en: War between Union and Confederacy over slavery; Union victory abolishes slavery and preserves national unity, accelerating industrialization.

events.desc_zh: 阿波羅11號太空人阿姆斯壯和艾德林成為首次踏上月球的人類，是人類太空探索的最大里程碑。
events.desc_en: Apollo 11 astronauts Armstrong and Aldrin become the first humans on the Moon — humanity's greatest milestone in space exploration.

events.desc_zh: 美國股市崩潰引發全球經濟大蕭條，失業率飆升，對全球政治和經濟格局產生深遠影響。
events.desc_en: US stock market crash triggers the global Great Depression; soaring unemployment profoundly reshapes world politics and economics.

events.desc_zh: 蘇聯解體後，美國成為冷戰後世界唯一的超級大國，主導新世界秩序。
events.desc_en: After the Soviet collapse, the US becomes the world's sole superpower, shaping the new world order.

events.desc_zh: 現代智人在非洲演化出現，非洲是全人類的發源地，基因研究證實所有現代人都源自非洲。
events.desc_en: Modern Homo sapiens evolves in Africa — the birthplace of all humanity, confirmed by genetic evidence tracing all modern humans to African origins.

events.desc_zh: 美尼斯王統一上下埃及，建立法老王朝，尼羅河流域文明進入鼎盛時期。
events.desc_en: King Menes unifies Upper and Lower Egypt, establishing the Pharaonic dynasties at the height of Nile Valley civilization.

events.desc_zh: 古夫法老下令建造的吉薩大金字塔，是古代世界七大奇蹟中唯一現存的，展示古埃及的工程成就。
events.desc_en: Built for Pharaoh Khufu, the Great Pyramid is the only surviving Wonder of the Ancient World, showcasing Egyptian engineering mastery.
events.amazon_title: The Oxford History of Ancient Egypt

events.desc_zh: 古埃及最後的鼎盛時期，以拉美西斯二世為代表，建造了阿布辛貝神廟等宏偉建築。
events.desc_en: Egypt's last great era of power, epitomized by Ramesses II; monumental constructions like Abu Simbel define this golden age.

events.zh: 埃及豔后克麗奧帕特拉
events.desc_zh: 最後一位法老，以與凱撒和安東尼的政治聯盟聞名，她的去世標誌著古埃及的終結，埃及成為羅馬行省。
events.desc_en: The last pharaoh, famous for alliances with Caesar and Antony; her death marks the end of ancient Egypt as it becomes a Roman province.

events.desc_zh: 腓尼基人在北非建立迦太基城邦，成為地中海西部最強大的商業帝國，與羅馬多次戰爭。
events.desc_en: Phoenicians found Carthage in North Africa, building the most powerful commercial empire in the western Mediterranean, later warring with Rome.

events.desc_zh: 阿拉伯軍隊征服埃及和北非，伊斯蘭文化和阿拉伯語在北非廣泛傳播，深刻改變了當地文化。
events.desc_en: Arab armies conquer Egypt and North Africa, spreading Islamic culture and Arabic language, profoundly transforming the region's culture.

events.desc_zh: 西非最強大的帝國，以黃金和鹽的貿易聞名，曼薩·穆薩統治時期是人類歷史上最富有的人之一。
events.desc_en: West Africa's most powerful empire, famed for gold and salt trade; Mansa Musa was one of the wealthiest individuals in history.

events.desc_zh: 衣索比亞高原的古代王國，是東非最重要的古代文明，與羅馬、波斯、印度有貿易往來，是最早採用基督教的國家之一。
events.desc_en: An ancient kingdom on the Ethiopian highlands, one of East Africa's most important civilizations; among the first to adopt Christianity.

events.desc_zh: 南部非洲最重要的古代文明，以巨石建築聞名，是繁盛的黃金貿易中心，津巴布韋國名由此而來。
events.desc_en: The most significant ancient civilization in southern Africa, known for massive stone enclosures; a prosperous gold trade center that gives Zimbabwe its name.

events.desc_zh: 歐洲殖民者從西非強行運走數百萬非洲人到美洲為奴，是人類歷史上規模最大的強迫遷徙之一，深刻影響非洲和美洲的歷史。
events.desc_en: European colonizers forcibly transported millions of Africans to the Americas; one of the largest forced migrations in history, profoundly shaping Africa and the Americas.

events.desc_zh: 歐洲列強在柏林召開會議，瓜分非洲大陸，確立殖民地邊界，大多數非洲現代國界由此而來。
events.desc_en: European powers meet in Berlin to partition Africa, establishing colonial boundaries that define most modern African borders.

events.desc_zh: 南非國民黨政府實施種族隔離政策，以法律強制分隔不同種族，受到國際社會廣泛譴責和制裁。
events.desc_en: The South African National Party enforces racial segregation by law, drawing widespread international condemnation and sanctions.

events.desc_zh: 1960年17個非洲國家宣告獨立，標誌著非洲去殖民化運動的高峰，改變了世界政治格局。
events.desc_en: In 1960, 17 African nations declare independence, marking the peak of African decolonization and reshaping world politics.

events.desc_zh: 納爾遜·曼德拉在南非首次全種族選舉中當選總統，結束種族隔離制度，成為和平與民主的象徵。
events.desc_en: Nelson Mandela wins South Africa's first fully democratic election, ending apartheid and becoming a global symbol of peace and democracy.

events.desc_zh: 胡圖族對圖西族展開大屠殺，約100天內80至100萬人遇難，是20世紀最嚴重的種族滅絕事件之一。
events.desc_en: Hutu extremists massacre the Tutsi population; 800,000–1,000,000 killed in 100 days — one of the worst genocides of the 20th century.

events.desc_zh: 詹姆斯·瓦特改良蒸汽機，成為工業革命的核心動力，從根本上改變了人類的生產方式。
events.desc_en: James Watt's improved steam engine powers the Industrial Revolution, fundamentally transforming human production.

events.desc_zh: 電報和電力技術的發展，開啟人類通訊和動力的新紀元，大幅縮短了距離。
events.desc_en: Development of the telegraph and electrical power opens a new era of communication and energy, dramatically shrinking distances.

events.desc_zh: 路易·巴斯德建立細菌致病理論，奠定現代醫學和公共衛生的基礎，是醫學史上最重要的突破之一。
events.desc_en: Louis Pasteur establishes germ theory, founding modern medicine and public health — one of medicine's most important breakthroughs.

events.desc_zh: 哈恩和斯特拉斯曼發現鈾的核分裂，開啟核能和核武器時代，深刻改變了20世紀的政治和能源格局。
events.desc_en: Hahn and Strassmann discover nuclear fission in uranium, opening the nuclear age and profoundly reshaping 20th-century politics and energy.

events.desc_zh: 圖靈的理論基礎和二戰後電子計算機的誕生，開啟了資訊時代，是人類歷史上最重要的技術革命之一。
events.desc_en: Turing's theoretical foundations and postwar electronic computers launch the information age — one of history's most important technological revolutions.

events.desc_zh: 大爆炸後10^-12秒，宇宙由夸克、膠子和輕子組成的熱湯構成，溫度高達萬億度。
events.desc_en: From 10^-12 seconds after the Big Bang, the universe consisted of a hot soup of quarks, gluons and leptons.

events.desc_zh: 大爆炸後約1微秒，夸克結合形成質子和中子，物質的基本單位誕生。
events.desc_en: About 1 microsecond after the Big Bang, quarks combined to form protons and neutrons.

events.desc_zh: 大爆炸後3-20分鐘，氫、氦、鋰等輕元素核形成，奠定宇宙化學組成（75%氫，25%氦）。
events.desc_en: 3-20 minutes after the Big Bang, light element nuclei formed, establishing the universe's chemical composition (75% hydrogen, 25% helium).

events.zh: 暗能量主導·宇宙加速膨脹
events.desc_zh: 約50億年前，暗能量開始主導宇宙，宇宙由減速膨脹轉為加速膨脹，最終命運可能是熱寂。
events.desc_en: About 5 billion years ago, dark energy began to dominate, causing the universe to accelerate in its expansion.

events.zh: 恆星核合成·重元素誕生
events.desc_zh: 第一代大質量恆星在超新星爆炸中合成鐵以上的重元素，播撒到宇宙中，形成下一代行星和生命的原料。
events.desc_en: First-generation massive stars synthesized heavy elements in supernova explosions, seeding the cosmos with the raw materials for planets and life.

events.desc_zh: 第一批恆星和星系的紫外線輻射將中性氫電離，宇宙從黑暗時代進入透明時代。
events.desc_en: UV radiation from the first stars and galaxies ionized neutral hydrogen, ending the cosmic Dark Ages.

events.desc_zh: 引力使星系聚集成星系群和星系團，形成宇宙的大尺度網狀結構。
events.desc_en: Gravity drew galaxies into groups and clusters, forming the large-scale web structure of the universe.

events.desc_zh: 銀河系在大爆炸後約3億年開始形成，由原始氣體雲和早期恆星構成，是宇宙最早的星系之一。
events.desc_en: The Milky Way began forming about 300 million years after the Big Bang, one of the universe's earliest galaxies.

events.desc_zh: 銀河系厚盤在約100億年前形成，包含較老的恆星族群，富含重元素。
events.desc_en: The Milky Way's thick disk formed about 10 billion years ago, containing older stellar populations rich in heavy elements.

events.zh: 蓋亞-恩克拉多斯星系合併
events.desc_zh: 銀河系與蓋亞-恩克拉多斯矮星系發生重大合併事件，大量恆星被吸收，改變了銀河系的結構。
events.desc_en: The Milky Way merged with the Gaia-Enceladus dwarf galaxy, absorbing billions of stars and reshaping its structure.

events.desc_zh: 銀河系薄盤在約80億年前形成，包含太陽等較年輕的恆星，是大多數恆星形成的地方。
events.desc_en: The thin disk formed about 8 billion years ago, containing younger stars like the Sun where most star formation occurs.

events.en: Solar System Born in Orion Arm
events.desc_zh: 太陽系在銀河系獵戶旋臂的分子雲中形成，距銀河系中心約2.6萬光年，每2.25億年繞銀心公轉一圈。
events.desc_en: The Solar System formed in a molecular cloud in the Milky Way's Orion Arm, about 26,000 light-years from the galactic center.

events.zh: 銀河系與仙女座星系碰撞（預測）
events.desc_zh: 仙女座星系正以每秒110公里的速度接近銀河系，預計約40億年後兩者將碰撞合併，形成新的橢圓星系。
events.desc_en: The Andromeda galaxy is approaching at 110 km/s and will collide with the Milky Way in about 4 billion years, forming a new elliptical galaxy.

events.desc_zh: 太陽形成後，剩餘氣體和塵埃形成旋轉的原行星盤，是所有行星、小行星和彗星的原材料。
events.desc_en: After the Sun formed, remaining gas and dust formed a rotating protoplanetary disk, the raw material for all planets, asteroids and comets.

events.zh: 木星大遷移（大飛馳假說）
events.desc_zh: 木星形成後向內遷移至火星軌道附近，又被土星拉回，此過程清空了內太陽系大量物質，解釋了火星偏小的原因。
events.desc_en: Jupiter migrated inward toward Mars's orbit before being pulled back by Saturn, sweeping the inner solar system and explaining Mars's small size.

events.desc_zh: 地球液態外核對流產生磁場，保護地球免受太陽風侵蝕，是生命存在的關鍵條件之一。
events.desc_en: Convection in Earth's liquid outer core generated a magnetic field, shielding it from solar wind — a key condition for life.

events.zh: 行星軌道重組（尼斯模型）
events.desc_zh: 外太陽系行星軌道發生大規模重組，海王星和天王星向外遷移，觸發晚期重轟炸期，大量小行星撞擊內太陽系。
events.desc_en: Massive reshuffling of outer planet orbits sent Neptune and Uranus outward, triggering the Late Heavy Bombardment.

events.desc_zh: 太陽開始穩定的氫核融合，進入約100億年的主序星階段，提供地球穩定的能量來源。
events.desc_en: The Sun began stable hydrogen fusion, entering its ~10 billion year main sequence phase, providing Earth with a stable energy source.

events.desc_zh: 海王星軌道外的冰質天體帶形成，是短週期彗星的來源，冥王星是其最大成員。
events.desc_en: The belt of icy bodies beyond Neptune's orbit formed, source of short-period comets; Pluto is its largest known member.

events.zh: 太陽膨脹為紅巨星（預測）
events.desc_zh: 約50億年後，太陽耗盡核心氫燃料，膨脹成紅巨星，半徑擴大至今日的200倍，地球將被吞噬。
events.desc_en: In about 5 billion years, the Sun will exhaust its hydrogen fuel and expand into a red giant, engulfing Earth.

events.desc_zh: 地球形成初期，頻繁的小行星撞擊使地表成為全球性的熔岩海洋，溫度超過1000度。
events.desc_en: In early Earth history, frequent asteroid impacts created a global magma ocean with temperatures exceeding 1000°C.

events.zh: 地球最早岩石（阿卡斯塔片麻岩）
events.desc_zh: 加拿大發現的阿卡斯塔片麻岩是地球現存最古老岩石，約40億年前形成，證明地殼早期存在。
events.desc_en: The Acasta Gneiss in Canada is Earth's oldest known intact crustal rock at ~4 billion years old.

events.zh: 二疊紀大滅絕·地球最嚴重物種浩劫
events.desc_zh: 約2.52億年前，96%的海洋物種和70%的陸地脊椎動物滅絕，可能由大規模火山噴發引發，是地球歷史上最嚴重的大滅絕。
events.desc_en: 96% of marine species and 70% of land vertebrates perished — the most severe mass extinction in Earth's history, likely triggered by massive volcanic eruptions.
events.wiki_zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/二疊紀-三疊紀滅絕事件

events.desc_zh: 約3400萬年前，大氣CO2濃度下降，南極冰蓋形成，地球進入更涼爽的冰室氣候，對全球洋流和氣候產生深遠影響。
events.desc_en: About 34 million years ago, falling CO2 levels allowed the Antarctic ice sheet to form, shifting Earth into a cooler icehouse climate.

events.desc_zh: 幼發拉底河和底格里斯河上游的新月沃土地區，人類開始系統性種植小麥、大麥並馴化山羊和綿羊，奠定文明基礎。
events.desc_en: In the Fertile Crescent of the upper Euphrates and Tigris, humans began systematic cultivation of wheat and barley and domesticated goats and sheep.

events.zh: 耶利哥城——世界最早城鎮
events.desc_zh: 位於今日巴勒斯坦的耶利哥是世界上持續有人居住最久的城鎮之一，約公元前8000年已有定居農業社區和防禦城牆。
events.desc_en: Located in present-day Palestine, Jericho is one of the world's oldest continuously inhabited towns, with settled agricultural communities and defensive walls by 8000 BCE.

events.zh: 歐貝德文化——神廟建築起源
events.desc_zh: 兩河流域南部的歐貝德文化以大型神廟建築和彩陶著稱，是後來蘇美文明的直接前身，神廟成為城市的核心。
events.desc_en: The Ubaid culture in southern Mesopotamia featured large temple buildings and painted pottery, the direct precursor to Sumerian civilization.

events.desc_zh: 幼發拉底河和底格里斯河流域發展出複雜的灌溉渠道系統，使乾旱地區能夠支持大規模農業和人口，奠定城市文明基礎。
events.desc_en: Complex irrigation canal systems in the Euphrates-Tigris basin enabled large-scale agriculture in arid regions, laying the foundation for urban civilization.

events.desc_zh: 烏魯克是世界第一個真正意義上的城市，人口達5萬，有複雜的行政、貿易和宗教體系，是文明的搖籃。
events.desc_en: Uruk was the world's first true city with a population of 50,000, featuring complex administrative, trade and religious systems.

events.desc_zh: 蘇美城邦以神廟為經濟中心，神廟管理土地、勞工和貿易，楔形文字最初就是為記錄神廟帳目而發明。
events.desc_en: Sumerian city-states centered their economies on temples that managed land, labor and trade; cuneiform writing was invented to record temple accounts.

events.desc_zh: 世界最早的文學作品，記述烏魯克王吉爾伽美什尋求永生的故事，包含大洪水神話（比聖經諾亞早1000年），反映蘇美人的宇宙觀。
events.desc_en: The world's earliest literary work, recounting King Gilgamesh's quest for immortality, containing a flood myth 1000 years older than Noah's story.

events.zh: 烏爾王陵——蘇美黃金文化
events.desc_zh: 烏爾王陵出土的黃金頭盔、豎琴和珠寶展示蘇美文明的高度藝術成就，也揭示了殉葬制度，數十名侍從與國王同葬。
events.desc_en: The Royal Tombs of Ur revealed stunning gold artifacts including helmets and lyres, showcasing Sumerian artistic achievement and human sacrifice practices.

events.desc_zh: 蘇美人發展出60進位制（今日時鐘和角度仍使用），能計算平方根和複利，記錄行星運動，奠定西方天文學基礎。
events.desc_en: Sumerians developed base-60 mathematics (still used in clocks and angles today), calculated square roots and compound interest, and recorded planetary movements.

events.zh: 烏爾納姆法典——最早成文法
events.en: Code of Ur-Nammu — Earliest Law Code
events.desc_zh: 烏爾第三王朝的烏爾納姆法典比漢摩拉比法典早300年，是現存最古老的成文法律，規定了刑事和民事的具體處罰。
events.desc_en: The Code of Ur-Nammu predates Hammurabi's by 300 years and is the oldest surviving written law code, specifying penalties for criminal and civil offenses.

events.zh: 阿摩利人入侵，烏爾滅亡
events.desc_zh: 來自敘利亞草原的阿摩利遊牧民族入侵兩河流域，烏爾第三王朝滅亡，兩河流域進入城邦割據時代。
events.desc_en: Amorite nomads from the Syrian steppe invaded Mesopotamia, ending the Ur III dynasty and ushering in an era of competing city-states.

events.desc_zh: 來自伊朗山區的喀西特人征服巴比倫，統治長達400年，是兩河流域被外族統治最長的時期，卻也吸收了巴比倫文化。
events.desc_en: The Kassites from the Iranian highlands conquered Babylon and ruled for 400 years — the longest foreign rule in Mesopotamia — while absorbing Babylonian culture.

events.desc_zh: 亞述王薩爾貢二世滅亡以色列北國，強制遷移數萬以色列人，形成「失落的十支派」，改變了猶太民族的歷史走向。
events.desc_en: Assyrian King Sargon II destroyed the northern Kingdom of Israel, deporting tens of thousands and creating the 'Ten Lost Tribes.'

events.desc_zh: 尼尼微的亞述巴尼拔圖書館收藏超過3萬塊楔形文字泥板，是古代最重要的知識中心，吉爾伽美什史詩的完整版在此發現。
events.desc_en: The Library of Ashurbanipal at Nineveh housed over 30,000 cuneiform tablets — the ancient world's greatest knowledge center where the complete Epic of Gilgamesh was found.

events.zh: 尼尼微陷落·亞述帝國滅亡
events.en: Fall of Nineveh — End of Assyria
events.desc_zh: 巴比倫與米底聯軍攻陷亞述首都尼尼微，稱霸兩河流域三百年的亞述帝國瞬間崩潰，新巴比倫帝國接管中東霸權。
events.desc_en: A Babylonian-Median coalition sacked Nineveh, instantly ending the Assyrian Empire that had dominated for 300 years.

events.zh: 尼布甲尼撒二世——新巴比倫極盛
events.desc_zh: 尼布甲尼撒二世重建巴比倫城，建造空中花園（古代七大奇蹟之一），征服猶大王國並摧毀耶路撒冷聖殿，猶太人被擄至巴比倫。
events.desc_en: Nebuchadnezzar II rebuilt Babylon, constructed the Hanging Gardens (one of the Seven Wonders), conquered Judah, and destroyed Jerusalem's Temple.

events.desc_zh: 古代世界七大奇蹟之一，傳說尼布甲尼撒二世為思念故鄉山林的王妃而建，層層疊疊的花園覆蓋整個宮殿，以複雜的灌溉系統澆水。
events.desc_en: One of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World, said to have been built by Nebuchadnezzar II for his homesick queen, featuring terraced gardens with complex irrigation.

events.zh: 巴比倫天文學黃金時代
events.desc_zh: 巴比倫天文學家精確預測日月蝕、行星運動，發展出黃道十二宮，計算出沙羅週期（18年日蝕循環），對希臘天文學影響深遠。
events.desc_en: Babylonian astronomers accurately predicted eclipses and planetary motions, developed the zodiac, and calculated the Saros cycle (18-year eclipse pattern), deeply influencing Greek astronomy.

events.zh: 居魯士令·猶太人獲釋回鄉
events.desc_zh: 波斯王居魯士大帝頒布詔令，允許被巴比倫擄走的猶太人返回迦南，重建耶路撒冷聖殿，被猶太人稱為解放者。
events.desc_en: Persian King Cyrus the Great decreed that Jews exiled by Babylon could return to Canaan and rebuild Jerusalem's Temple, hailed by Jews as a liberator.

events.zh: 塞琉古帝國——希臘化兩河流域
events.desc_zh: 亞歷山大大帝死後，部將塞琉古建立帝國統治兩河流域，希臘文化與東方文明深度融合，形成獨特的希臘化文明。
events.desc_en: After Alexander's death, general Seleucus established an empire over Mesopotamia, creating a unique fusion of Greek and Eastern civilizations.

events.desc_zh: 伊朗遊牧民族帕提亞人建立帝國，奪回塞琉古的統治，長期與羅馬帝國對抗，控制絲路貿易，是東西方交流的樞紐。
events.desc_en: The Iranian Parthians founded an empire, reclaiming Seleucid territory and rivaling Rome for centuries while controlling Silk Road trade.

events.en: Baghdad Founded — Center of the World
events.desc_zh: 阿拔斯哈里發曼蘇爾在底格里斯河畔建立圓形城市巴格達，很快成為世界最大城市（人口超過100萬），是伊斯蘭黃金時代的核心。
events.desc_en: Abbasid Caliph al-Mansur built the circular city of Baghdad on the Tigris, which quickly became the world's largest city (over 1 million) and heart of the Islamic Golden Age.

events.desc_zh: 巴格達智慧宮將希臘、波斯、印度的科學著作譯成阿拉伯文，保存並發展了古典知識，代數學、光學、醫學在此大幅進步。
events.desc_en: Baghdad's House of Wisdom translated Greek, Persian and Indian scientific texts into Arabic, preserving classical knowledge and advancing algebra, optics and medicine.

events.zh: 賽克斯-皮科協議·秘密瓜分中東
